definition.en: Understand that awareness is the criteria, however. There is no law limiting the number of psychological structures available to you, but because of your present development, and because of this alone, you are hampered.
definition.zh: 然而，要明白「觉知」是标准。没有任何法则限制你所能获得的心理结构的数量，但仅仅由于你目前的发展，你受到了阻碍。

definition.en: In greater terms you are just as awake when you are asleep, but the focus of your awareness is turned in other directions.
definition.zh: 从更大的角度来说，你在睡眠时同样清醒，只是你的觉知焦点转向了其他方向。

definition.en: Already Miss Callahan's vital core of awareness is appearing on another plane, and if you will forgive an analogy, she appears there as a wondering but not frightened young girl.
definition.zh: 卡拉汉小姐的觉知核心已经出现在另一个层面，如果你允许我用一个类比，她在那里就像一个好奇但并不害怕的年轻女孩。

definition.en: These projections take a strong propelling force, for you are literally traveling from one dimension or reality to another. And yet you will retain awareness.
definition.zh: 这些投射需要强大的推进力，因为你实际上是从一个维度或实相旅行到另一个。然而，你将保留觉知。

definition.en: Consciousness is not made up of balances so much as it is made up of exquisite imbalances, and the focus of awareness is to some degree the result of this state of excitability.
definition.zh: 意识与其说是由平衡构成，不如说是由精妙的不平衡构成，而觉知的焦点在某种程度上就是这种兴奋状态的结果。

explanation: 觉知是意识的多维感知能力，并非局限于日常清醒状态下的单一认知。它包含多个层次：细胞层面的基础觉知、潜意识层面的综合觉知、以及内在自我（内我）对整体人格的觉知。内我具有最高层次的自我意识，同时知晓意识与潜意识、神经系统运作及身体功能，并统筹全局。人的完整身份是跨维度的，同时存在于多个时空，其中一部分能同时记忆不同维度的体验。觉知可以扩展，通过接触内在自我，人能超越当前自我限制，觉察到多重人格和多重存在。这种扩展不是分裂，而是对整体身份的更深认知——每个自我在保持独立性的同时，也能以更高觉知参与整体。觉知本质上是动态的，它通过意识脉冲在不同现实间流动，使个体能同时在不同系统中体验和创造。

quotes.text: The concepts in such a system as this can help break those barriers. There are, then, stratas of consciousness existing at once. The ones you are not aware of yet seem more progressed, developed than your own. You are a part of them now. You can know them as you begin to stretch your concepts of personhood and awareness. In terms of time you have many bodies, as you are born and reborn in earth experience. Your consciousness straddles those existences, and even the atoms and molecules within your present body contain the coded knowledge of those other (really simultaneous) forms. These units of consciousness are within all physical matter, containing their own memories. Both biologically and psychically, then, you are aware of your multipersonhood.
quotes.translation: 这类体系中的概念能帮助打破那些障碍。因此，存在同时运作的意识层次。你尚未觉察的那些层次，似乎比你自己的层次更进步、更发达。你现在就是它们的一部分。当你开始扩展对人格和觉察的概念时，你就能认识它们。就时间而言，你拥有许多身体，因为你在尘世经验中不断出生和重生。你的意识跨越了那些存在，甚至你当前身体内的原子和分子，也包含着那些其他（实际上是同时的）形态的编码知识。这些意识单位存在于所有物质中，携带着它们自己的记忆。因此，无论从生物学还是灵性角度，你都觉察到你的多重人格性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 683
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The individual involved is aware of very little as far as physical objects are concerned. There is, for example, a table in front of him. The table is real, it is physical. Under ordinary circumstances it could be seen and touched. Objects could be placed upon it; and yet, Doctor Instream, our entranced individual is not conscious of that table. In his state he is concentrating upon some object which we cannot see. Now, consider: we will attempt to prove the existence of this material table to this individual who is not aware of it. How, therefore, could we prove to him that this table exists, when he is not aware of it in any manner whatsoever? His attentions are focused elsewhere. For him the table does not exist. We have indeed a rather delightful dilemma; and yet, is this not what you require of me? I speak of "you" simply because I have come in contact with you. I recognize only too well your sympathy and your understanding. Nevertheless the situation in which you put me is exactly like the situation which I have only now described.
quotes.translation: 就物质对象而言，涉及的个体几乎觉察不到什么。例如，他面前有一张桌子。桌子是真实的，是物质的。在正常情况下，它可见可触，物品可以放在上面；然而，因斯特里姆博士，我们这位出神状态的个体却意识不到那张桌子。在他的状态中，他正专注于某个我们看不见的对象。现在，思考一下：我们将试图向这个未觉察的个体证明这张物质桌子的存在。那么，当他无论如何都觉察不到它时，我们如何向他证明这张桌子存在呢？他的注意力集中在别处。对他来说，桌子并不存在。我们确实面临一个相当有趣的困境；然而，这不正是你们要求我做的吗？我说“你们”，仅仅是因为我与你们接触过。我太了解你们的同情和理解。尽管如此，你们让我陷入的处境，恰恰与我刚才描述的情况一模一样。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Think of the greater you -- call it the entity if you want to -- as forming a psychic structure quite as real as your physical one, but composed of many selves. As each cell of your body has its position within your corporeal space and boundaries, so each self within the entity is aware of its own "time" and dimension of activity. The body is a temporal structure. The cells, however, while a part of this body, are not aware of the entire dimension in which your consciousness dwells. They do not perceive all of the elements that are available even in three-dimensional experience, yet your present consciousness -- seemingly so much more sophisticated -- physically rests upon cellular awareness.
quotes.translation: 想想那个更大的你——如果你愿意，可以称之为存有——它形成了一个灵性结构，像你的物质结构一样真实，但由许多自我组成。正如你身体的每个细胞在你肉体空间和边界内各有其位置，存有中的每个自我也觉察到自己的“时间”和活动维度。身体是一个时间性结构。然而，细胞虽然是身体的一部分，却觉察不到你意识所居的整个维度。它们感知不到即使在三维经验中可用的所有元素，然而你当前的意识——看似复杂得多——在物质上却依赖于细胞的觉察。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The overall needs of the personality are always taken into consideration by what I have called in the past the inner ego. Now this portion of the self is indeed self-conscious in the highest meaning of the term--aware of the subconscious portion of the personality, aware of the primary conscious framework that you call your ego, and constantly directs the overall activities. This portion is aware of the complicated workings of the nervous system and all bodily functions. It is the overseer. It knows when to allow subconscious needs and wishes their fulfillment. It knows when to put fulfillment off for a time. It is this part of the personality that is in charge of overall stability.
quotes.translation: 人格的整体需求始终被我过去所称的内在自我所考虑。现在，自我的这一部分确实是这个词最高意义上的自我意识——觉察到人格的潜意识部分，觉察到你称之为自我的主要意识框架，并持续指导整体活动。这一部分觉察到神经系统的复杂运作和所有身体功能。它是监督者。它知道何时允许潜意识需求和愿望得到满足。它知道何时暂时推迟满足。正是人格的这一部分负责整体稳定性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: You are truly multidimensional personalities, as I have said before. At some point in your development you will become more and more aware of the true nature of your identity. There is, for example, a part of you who is very aware of the pulsations you have just been discussing, and who is aware of the pulse-like nature of memory. When the pulse is in this physical reality, then you, as you know yourselves, have memory of this existence. When the pulse is in another dimension, there is memory of that existence. Now a portion of your entire identity has memory of both. The entire personality structure dwells in many dimensions, and simultaneously.
quotes.translation: 正如我之前所说，你们确实是多维人格。在你们发展的某个阶段，你们会越来越觉察到自己身份的真实本质。例如，你的一部分非常觉察你们刚才讨论的脉动，并觉察到记忆的脉动性质。当脉动在这个物质实相中时，那么你们，如你们所知的自己，就拥有对这一存在的记忆。当脉动在另一个维度中时，就拥有对那个存在的记忆。现在，你们整体身份的一部分拥有两者的记忆。整个的人格结构同时存在于许多维度中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The physical body is therefore an attribute of consciousness. The atoms and molecules possess a self-consciousness to some degree, as well as a generalized consciousness. The self awareness of a human being is partially composed of the combination of self aware individual atoms and molecules that compose it. The generalized consciousness which you call the subconscious, is composed of the combination of generalized consciousness from the individual cells and molecules, but now we come down to the source of the self aware conscious individual behind or within each molecule. Where did it come from to begin with?
quotes.translation: 因此，物质身体是意识的一个属性。原子和分子在某种程度上拥有自我意识，以及一种普遍意识。人类的自我觉察部分地由组成它的自我觉察的个体原子和分子的组合构成。你称之为潜意识的普遍意识，由来自个体细胞和分子的普遍意识的组合构成，但现在我们回到每个分子背后或内部那个自我觉察的个体意识的源头。它最初从哪里来？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Your experiments with the pendulum are quite helpful to you, in that the subconscious is allowed, through its own action, to make itself more readily available. Your own conscious awareness is increased because you are then aware of inner actions with which the conscious mind had not been familiar. Here we have a coming together of actions, a joining and an immersion of one action within another: the action of the subconscious in answering questions put to it by the conscious mind, （use brain rather than mind）, and the acceptance, which is itself action on the part of consciousness, of the answers received.
quotes.translation: 你使用钟摆进行的实验对你很有帮助，因为潜意识通过自身的运作，得以更顺畅地展现自己。你的意识觉察力也因此增强，因为你开始意识到那些意识心智此前并不熟悉的内在活动。这里涉及多种行动的汇聚、交融与沉浸：潜意识回答意识心智（此处用大脑而非心智）所提问题的行动，以及意识本身接受这些答案的行动——接受本身也是一种行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: That is, not only would they be aware of what seems to you to be past, present and future within your system, but they would be aware of several other systems and be able to function within them simultaneously, gaining and creating experience within all of these systems, even while maintaining overall identity. They would be conscious, for example, of themselves as, say, an entity, and simultaneously they would be aware of separate existences as individuals in various systems. This is as far as we can carry this at present.
quotes.translation: 也就是说，他们不仅会觉察到你们系统中看似属于过去、现在和未来的事物，还会觉察到其他多个系统，并能在这些系统中同时运作，在所有系统中获取并创造经验，同时保持整体身份。例如，他们会意识到自己作为一个存有存在，同时也会觉察到自己在不同系统中作为个体的独立存在。目前我们只能阐述到这一步。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: We must use analogies. It can be thought of as the most exterior projection of the inner ego. When you find yourself, so to speak, watching what you think of as the ego, then you are in contact with this portion of the self. It is aware of both conscious and subconscious motivations and realities, and it is also aware of projections into other fields of actuality. I have hinted before of these matters, for when I say that you will use this other portion of the self to examine waking consciousness and probe beneath it, I already presuppose a you that uses this self. In other words, you are already magnifying the limitations of the self and extending them.
quotes.translation: 我们必须使用类比。这可以被视为内在自我最外层的投射。当你仿佛在观察你所认为的自我时，你便接触到了自我的这一部分。它既觉察到意识与潜意识的动机和实相，也觉察到向其他实相领域的投射。我之前曾暗示过这些事，因为当我说你将运用自我的这一部分来检视清醒意识并深入探究其下时，我已经预设了一个运用这一自我的"你"。换句话说，你正在放大自我的局限，并拓展其边界。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Individual selves will retain identity. They will be able to participate at a high level of awareness with the whole of which they are part. The whole is far more infinite than you can conceive. In a basic sense, the whole is aware of all of its parts, and in a basic senseall of its parts are aware of the whole. But each self must go its own way and develop its own abilities and explore the possibilities which it creates itself, otherwise the whole would stagnate.
quotes.translation: 个体自我将保留身份。它们能够以高度觉察力参与其所归属的整体。整体远比你能想象的更为无限。从根本意义上说，整体觉察其所有部分，而所有部分也从根本上觉察整体。但每个自我必须走自己的路，发展自身能力，探索自己创造的可能性，否则整体就会停滞。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: We will resume dictation. (Pause; one of many.) Consciousness has many characteristics, some of course known to you. Many of the characteristics of consciousness, however, are not so apparent, since presently you largely use your own consciousness in such a way that its perceptions appear in quite other than "natural" guises. You are aware of your own consciousness, in other words, through the medium of your own physical mechanism. You are not nearly as aware of your own consciousness when it is not operating primarily through the mediumship of the body, as it does in out-of-body states and some dissociated conditions.
quotes.translation: 我们将继续口述。（停顿；多次之一。）意识具有许多特性，其中一些你们自然知晓。然而，意识的许多特性并不那么明显，因为目前你们主要使用自身意识的方式，使其感知以远非"自然"的面貌呈现。换句话说，你们是通过自身物理机制的中介来觉察自己的意识。当意识不主要通过身体媒介运作时——例如在出体状态和某些解离状态中——你们对自身意识的觉察就远没有那么清晰。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In other systems this is not necessarily the case. The time system within them is entirely different than your own. In these the inner self is aware of itself as more than one ego. (Pause.) The inner self can play more than one role at once, consciously in other words. Simply as an analogy, it would be as if within physical reality you lived, say, the life of a rich man of great talent, the life of a poor man with entirely different talents, and the life of a mother and career woman. You would be aware of yourself in each of these three roles, and find qualities being developed in each of the separate lives.
quotes.translation: 在其他系统中，情况未必如此。其中的时间系统与你们的完全不同。在这些系统中，内在自我觉察到自身不止一个自我。（停顿。）内在自我可以同时扮演多个角色，换句话说，是有意识地扮演。简单类比来说，就像在物质实相中，你同时过着一位才华横溢的富人的生活、一位天赋迥异的穷人的生活，以及一位母亲兼职业女性的生活。你会觉察到自己在这三个角色中的存在，并发现每个独立生命中都在发展不同的品质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The inner senses, though I have in the past described them by separating their functions and characteristics, basically operate together in such a way that in your terms it would be highly difficult to separate one from the others. They function with a perfect spontaneous order, aware of all synchronicities. In that psychological universe, then, it is possible for entities "to be everywhere at once," aware of everything at once. Your world is composed of such "entities"--the units of consciousness that form your body. The kinds of conscious minds that you have cannot hold that kind of information.
quotes.translation: 内在感官虽在过去我曾按其功能与特性分别描述，但它们本质上协同运作，在你看来极难彼此区分。它们以完美的自发秩序运作，觉察所有共时性事件。在那个心理宇宙中，实体能够"同时存在于各处"，同时觉察一切。你的世界正是由这些"实体"——构成你身体的意识单位——所组成。你所拥有的那种意识心无法容纳这类信息。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... You identify a highly evolved self-consciousness with your own species development, and with your own kind of perceptive mechanisms. You apply these as rules or conditions whenever you examine any other kind of life. In your system of probabilities there are no reptilian men or women, yet in other probabilities they do indeed exist. I mention this only to show you that the evolutionary system you recognize is but one such system. (Intently:) The physical basis rests latently within your own cellular structure, however. You think that evolution is finished. Its impetus, however, comes from within the nature of consciousness itself. It always has. In some quarters it is fashionable these days to say that man's consciousness is now an element in a new kind of evolution -- but that "new consciousness" has always been inherent. You are only now beginning to recognize its existence. Every consciousness is aware of itself as itself.5 Each consciousness, then, is self-aware. It may not be self-aware in the same way that you are. It may not reflect upon its own condition. On the other hand, it may have no need to.
quotes.translation: 请稍候……你将高度进化的自我意识等同于你们物种的发展，以及你们特有的感知机制。当你检视任何其他生命形态时，便将这些作为规则或条件加以应用。在你们的概率系统中不存在爬虫人，但在其他概率中它们确实存在。我提及这点仅为说明，你们所认知的进化系统只是众多系统之一。（专注地：）然而，物理基础潜藏于你们自身的细胞结构之中。你们认为进化已经完成，但其动力始终源于意识本身的本质。如今在某些领域，流行说法是人类的意识已成为新型进化中的要素——但那种"新意识"始终是内在固有的。你们只是现在才开始认识到它的存在。每个意识都觉察到自身即是自身。每个意识因此都是自我觉察的。它可能不以你那样的方式自我觉察，可能不反思自身状态，但另一方面，它或许根本无需如此。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 705
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The entity, the true multidimensional self, is aware of all of its experiences, and this knowledge is to some extent available to these other portions of the self, including of course the physical self as you know it. These various portions of the self in fact will eventually (in your terms) become fully aware. Period. This awareness will automatically alter what now seems to be their nature, and add to the multiplicitude of existence.
quotes.translation: 存有——真正的多维自性——觉察自身所有经验，这种认知在某种程度上可供自性的其他部分获取，当然也包括你所知的物质自性。事实上（以你们的时间观），自性的这些不同部分终将完全觉醒。句号。这种觉察将自动改变它们目前看似本质的特性，并增添存在的多重性。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: At certain levels the brain can handle simultaneous material, of course, even though you may be conscious of only a smattering of it. The body is aware of multitudinous simultaneous stimuli that consciously escape you, and is able to act on the information. This includes all kinds of sense data that are not consciously pertinent. (Intently:) Because of the particular kind of ego-orientation that the race decided upon, however, many probabilities of development inherent in the species have been latent. Inherently the physical brain is capable of dealing with more than one main line of consciousness. This does not mean the development of dual personality, by the way. It means the further expansion of the concept of identity: "You" would not only be aware of the you that you have always known, in the same way that you are now, but a deeper sense of identity would also arise.
quotes.translation: 在特定层面，大脑当然能处理同步信息，即便你仅能意识到其中零星片段。身体觉察着无数同步刺激——这些刺激在意识层面被你忽略——并能依据信息采取行动。这包括所有在意识层面不相关的感官数据。（专注地：）然而，由于人类种族所决定的特定自我导向，物种内在的许多发展概率一直处于潜伏状态。本质上，物质大脑能够处理不止一条主要意识线。顺便说一句，这并非指发展双重人格，而是指身份概念的进一步扩展："你"不仅会以现在的方式觉察到一直所知的自己，还会产生更深刻的身份感。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

source_raw.title: SESSION 692

quotes.text: All of this presented a quite erroneous picture. Physically speaking, earth itself has its own kind of gestalt consciousness. If you must, then think of that earth consciousness as grading (spelled) upward in great slopes of awareness from relatively "inert" particles of dust and stone through the mineral, vegetable, and animal kingdoms. Even then, remember that those kingdoms are not so separate after all. Each one is highly related to each of the others. Nothing happens in one such kingdom that does not affect the others. A great, gracious cooperation exists between those seemingly separate systems, however. If you will remember that even atoms and molecules have consciousness, then it will be easier for you to understand that there is indeed a certain kind of awareness that unites these kingdoms.
quotes.translation: 这一切造成了相当错误的图景。从物理层面而言，地球本身拥有其独特的完形意识。若你坚持，可将地球意识想象为从相对"惰性"的尘埃与岩石粒子，经由矿物、植物、动物王国，向上延伸至意识的大斜坡。即便如此，请记住这些王国并非真正分离。每个王国都与其他王国高度关联。任何王国中发生的事件都会影响其他王国。然而，这些看似分离的系统之间存在着伟大的、仁慈的合作。若你记得连原子和分子都拥有意识，便更容易理解确实存在某种联结这些王国的觉察。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 705
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Some people are only aware of -- or largely aware of -- impulses toward anger, because they have inhibited those natural impulses toward love that would otherwise temper what seemed to be aggressive desires. When you begin trusting yourselves, you start by taking it for granted that to some extent at least you have not trusted yourself or your impulses in the past: You have thought that impulses were dangerous, disruptive, or even evil. So as you begin to learn self-trust, you acknowledge your impulses. You try them on for size. You see where they lead you by allowing them some freedom. You do not follow urges through that would hurt others physically, or that seem in direct contradiction to your present beliefs -- but you do acknowledge them. You do try to discover their source. Behind them you will almost always find an inhibited impulse -- or many of them -- that motivated you to move in some ideal direction, to seek a love or understanding so idealized in your mind that it seemed impossible to achieve. You are left with the impulse to strike out.
quotes.translation: 有些人只觉察到——或主要觉察到——愤怒的冲动，因为他们抑制了那些本可缓和看似攻击性欲望的自然之爱冲动。当你开始信任自己时，首先要承认：至少在一定程度上，你过去未曾信任自己或自己的冲动——你认为冲动是危险的、破坏性的，甚至邪恶的。因此，当你开始学习自我信任时，要承认你的冲动。试穿它们看看是否合身。通过给予它们一定自由，观察它们将你引向何方。你不会遵循那些会伤害他人身体、或与你当前信念直接矛盾的冲动——但你会承认它们。你会试图发现它们的源头。在它们背后，你几乎总会发现一个被抑制的冲动——或许多个——这些冲动曾激励你朝着某个理想方向前进，去寻求你心中如此理想化以至于看似不可能实现的爱或理解。最终你只剩下向外发泄的冲动。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

SESSION 872, AUGUST 8, 1979
9:15 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: The inner ego knows when to apply safety valves, and is aware of the danger before the outer ego is alerted. The inner ego is concerned with maintaining the foundations and balance, which is very important, of the whole self, and it is open to messages from the overall entity. The inner ego receives messages through the inner senses, and is aware of realities which the outer ego cannot afford to recognize because of its specialization. In some important aspects the outer ego is supposed to represent to some degree the subdominant personalities who still dwell in the subconscious. When the outer ego is narrow, and poorly represents these subdominant personalities then they rise up in arms, and when conditions are favorable attempt to express themselves through a momentary weakness on the part of the dominant ego. But without even doing this they may momentarily take over or express themselves through a single function, such as speech or motion, while the outer ego is blissfully unaware.
quotes.translation: 内在自我知道何时运用安全阀，并在外在自我警觉之前就已意识到危险。内在自我关注的是维持整个自体的根基与平衡——这极为重要——并且它对来自整体存有的讯息保持开放。内在自我通过内在感官接收讯息，并意识到那些因专业化而无法被外在自我承认的实相。在某些重要方面，外在自我本应在某种程度上代表那些仍栖居于潜意识中的从属人格。当外在自我狭隘且未能充分代表这些从属人格时，它们便会奋起反抗；一旦条件有利，它们就会试图通过主导自我的瞬间弱点来表达自己。但即便不这样做，它们也可能暂时接管或通过单一功能（如言语或动作）来表达自己，而外在自我却浑然不觉。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The conscious mind was [therefore] expected to perform alone, so to speak, ignoring the highly intuitive inner information that is also available to it. It was not supposed to be aware of such data. Yet any individual knows quite well that intuitive hunches, inspiration, precognitive information or clairvoyant material has often risen to conscious knowledge. Usually it is shoved away and disregarded because you have been taught that the conscious mind should not hold with such "nonsense." So you have been told to trust your conscious mind, while at the same time you were led to believe it could only be aware of stimuli that came to it from the outside physical world.
quotes.translation: 因此，可以说意识心智被期望独自运作，忽略同样可被其获取的高度直觉性的内在信息。它本不应意识到这类数据。然而，任何个体都相当清楚，直觉预感、灵感、预知信息或千里眼素材常常上升到意识知识层面。通常这些会被推开并忽视，因为你被教导意识心智不应理会这种"胡言乱语"。所以你被告知要信任自己的意识心智，同时又被引导相信它只能意识到来自外部物理世界的刺激。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The private psyche, the part of you that you do not recognize, is aware of those patterns even as it is aware of private physical biological patterns about which it forms your image. Certain leanings, inclinations, and probabilities are present then in your biological structure, to be triggered or not according to your purposes and intents. You may personally have the ability to be a fine athlete, for example. Yet your inclinations and intents may carry you in a different direction, so that the necessary triggers are not activated. Each individual is gifted in a variety of ways. His or her own desires and beliefs activate certain abilities and ignore others.
quotes.translation: 私密的心灵——你不认识的那部分自己——意识到那些模式，正如它意识到那些构成你形象的私密生理生物模式。某些倾向、偏好和可能性因此存在于你的生物结构中，根据你的目的和意图被触发或不被触发。例如，你个人可能具备成为优秀运动员的能力，但你的倾向和意图可能将你引向不同方向，以致必要的触发机制未被激活。每个个体都以多种方式被赋予天赋，其自身的欲望和信念会激活某些能力而忽略其他。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It has been truthfully said that the "unconscious" is intimately aware of the most minute details of your health, state of mind, and relationships. "It" is also aware of the state of the earth's health -- even of the environmental conditions on the other side of the planet. It is familiar with the cultural climate also. Your recognized consciousness operates as it does because of immense information-gathering procedures -- procedures that unite all species. Biologically such information is coded, but that physical information, such as in the genes and chromosomes, can be altered through experience and mental activity in other species as well as your own.
quotes.translation: 诚然，"无意识"密切知晓你健康、心态和关系中最微小的细节。"它"也知晓地球的健康状态——甚至地球另一边的环境状况。它还熟悉文化气候。你被认可的意识之所以如此运作，是因为庞大的信息收集程序——这些程序联合了所有物种。生物层面上，这类信息被编码，但那些物理信息（如基因和染色体中的信息）可以通过经验与心智活动在其它物种及你自身物种中被改变。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I shall of course take advantage of the opportunity, but then you will have time to speak to me at your leisure. There are many points to be considered, and these matters certainly cannot be covered in one evening such as this. I hinted at our last discussion that it is indeed within the ability of the human personality to become aware of other realities while still keeping contact with physical reality. Manipulation in the physical universe is of course a necessity, but there are ways by which the human individual can become aware of other quite valid realities, and still maintain balance and control within his own more usual field of activity.
quotes.translation: 我当然会利用这个机会，但随后你将有闲暇时间与我交谈。有许多要点需要考虑，这些事显然无法在这样一个晚上全部涵盖。我在上次讨论中暗示过，人类人格确实有能力在保持与物理实相接触的同时，意识到其他实相。在物质宇宙中的操控当然是必要的，但存在某些方式，使人类个体能够意识到其他同样有效的实相，同时在其更惯常的活动领域中维持平衡与控制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The tree is also inner aware of its environment to an astonishing degree. It maintains contact awareness and the ability to manipulate itself in two completely different worlds, so to speak, one in which it meets little resistance growing upward, and one composed of much heavier elements into which it must grow downward. Man needs artificial methods for example to operate effectively on land or in water, but the so-called unconscious tree manages very nicely in two worlds as diverse certainly as land and water, and makes himself a part of each. I am speaking now of a tree as a "he" for reasons that I will go into in a further discussion.
quotes.translation: 树木对其环境的内部觉知也达到惊人程度。它保持着接触觉知，并在两个截然不同的世界中维持自我操控的能力——可以说，一个世界它向上生长时几乎遇不到阻力，另一个则由更沉重的元素构成，它必须向下生长其中。人类需要人工方法才能有效在陆地或水中运作，而所谓无意识的树木却能在两个无疑如陆地与水般迥异的世界中从容应对，并使自己成为每个世界的一部分。我现在将树木称为"他"，原因将在后续讨论中详述。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Understand that awareness is the criteria, however. There is no law limiting the number of psychological structures available to you, but because of your present development, and because of this alone, you are hampered. Experience or lack of it on various fields has not yet been possible, so practically speaking you have a limited number of basic psychological structures to deal with; and your perception, clear psychological understanding, intuitive comprehension of, and manipulation and psychological constructions of these basic structures, will determine the validity of your material constructions that will then form your environment.
quotes.translation: 然而，请理解觉知才是关键标准。并无任何法则限制你可运用的心理结构数量，但受限于你当前的发展阶段——且仅因此故——你受到束缚。由于尚未能在不同领域积累经验或缺乏经验，实际上你能处理的基本心理结构数量有限；而你对这些基本结构的感知、清晰的心理认知、直觉领悟、操控及心理建构，将决定你物质建构的有效性，这些建构继而会形成你的环境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: It is very easy of course to expand our analogy, changing the radio to a television set. In this case the projections on the screen would be fully dimensional, aware of each viewer in each living room. (Pause.) Not only this, but the screen people would understand the relationship between you the viewer, and, say, the other viewers in the same town. Behind the scenes not only would the performers, as performers in all of the programs, all know each other, but the characters portrayed by them would know each other and be aware of each other's roles in the programs, and even now and then stray into one another's dramas.
quotes.translation: 当然，我们很容易将类比扩展，把收音机换成电视机。此时屏幕上的投影将具有全息维度，能感知每个客厅里的每位观众。（停顿）不仅如此，屏幕中的人物还会理解你这位观众与同城其他观众之间的关系。在幕后，不仅所有节目的表演者彼此相识，就连他们所扮演的角色也相互认识，并知晓彼此在节目中的定位，甚至偶尔会闯入彼此的剧情。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Another thing. It is in some ways convenient that you are not consciously aware of each breath that you take. But it is sheer stupidity to ignore the inner self which does the breathing, and is aware of the mechanics involved. What you almost get here is that some little unknown self performs these necessary functions, and that is not the case. I have said that the mind is a part of the inner world, but you have access to your own minds which you ignore, and this access would lead you inevitably to the truths about the physical world. Working inward you could understand the outward so much more clearly.
quotes.translation: 另有一事。从某些角度看，你未能有意识地察觉每次呼吸，这倒也方便。但若忽视那个进行呼吸、并知晓其中运作机制的内在自我，则纯属愚昧。这里几乎要造成误解：仿佛有个无名的小我执行着这些必要功能——事实并非如此。我曾说过，心智属于内在世界的一部分，但你们本可触及却被忽视的自身心智，这种触及必将引领你们发现物质世界的真相。向内探索，你反而能更清晰地理解外在世界。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Nevertheless, the atoms and molecules within the nail do possess their own kind of consciousness. The atoms and molecules that make up the pages of this book are also, within their own level, aware. Nothing exists -- neither rock, mineral, plant, animal, or air -- that is not filled with consciousness of its own kind. So you stand amid a constant vital commotion, a gestalt of aware energy, and you are yourselves physically composed of conscious cells that carry within themselves the realization of their own identity, that cooperate willingly to form the corporeal structure that is your physical body.
quotes.translation: 然而，指甲中的原子与分子确实拥有其自身的意识。构成这本书页面的原子与分子，在其自身层面也具备觉知。没有任何存在——无论是岩石、矿物、植物、动物还是空气——不充满其自身种类的意识。因此你始终置身于持续的生命骚动中，一个觉知能量的完形，而你们自身在物质层面由具有意识的细胞组成，这些细胞内在携带着对自身身份的认知，并自愿合作形成构成你肉体的物质结构。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: （For while approaching the end of the exercise routine I am quite aware that I will be in an extremely relaxed mood, and that as I lay resting I can count on drifting into a state where dreams reveal themselves. This would include other related material like hypnagogic images, hearing voices, etc. My point is that I am usually aware that such passages are transpiring as I lay quietly; I am not asleep yet some of the material seems to be dreams. This is a long way from being fully and consciously active while such internal data is manifesting itself, but perhaps it is a beginning.
quotes.translation: （因为当接近练习常规的尾声时，我清楚意识到自己将处于极度放松的状态，且当我躺下休息时，可以预期会滑入梦境显现的状态。这包括其他相关素材，如入睡前意象、听见声音等。我的重点在于：当我静静躺着时，通常能觉察到这些过程正在发生；我尚未入睡，但某些素材似乎已是梦境。这距离在内在数据显现时保持完全清醒的主动意识状态还很遥远，但或许是个开端。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: The ego is not aware of the manner in which dreams are created. Neither is it aware of the manner in which the inner self creates physical matter. Neither, therefore, is it aware of those distortions that cause it to construct faults within physical matter, for in all cases the physical matter of the human body will be subconsciously created in line with inner conditions.
quotes.translation: 自我并不知晓梦境被创造的方式，也不知晓内在自我创造物质的方式。因此，它同样无法察觉那些导致它在物质中构建缺陷的扭曲——因为在所有情况下，人类身体的物质都将根据内在状态被潜意识地创造出来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The purpose of the Speakers is to help you correlate and understand this multidimensional existence, and to bring as much as possible of it to your conscious attention. Only by learning to feel, or sense, or intuitively perceive the depths of your own experience can you glimpse the nature of All That Is. By becoming more aware of your consciousness as it operates in physical life, you can learn to watch it as it manipulates through these other less familiar areas. Probable realities are only probable to you because you are not aware of them.
quotes.translation: 演讲者的目的是帮助你们关联并理解这种多维存在，并尽可能将其带入你们的意识觉知。只有学会感受、觉察或直觉感知自身经验的深度，你们才能一窥"一切万有"的本质。通过更清楚地觉察意识在物质生活中的运作方式，你们可以学会观察它如何操纵穿越这些较不熟悉的领域。可能实相对你们而言之所以只是可能，正是因为你们尚未觉察到它们。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now. You have become somewhat aware of the subconscious. This means that your ego has enlarged its conception of the self, of itself, to include certain activities of which it is now aware. Progress in the development of personality in the long run will be determined by the ability of the whole self to recognize and become aware of all of its self-conscious portions.
quotes.translation: 现在。你们已对潜意识略有觉察。这意味着你们的自我已扩展了自身对"自己"的概念，将某些它现在觉察到的活动纳入其中。从长远来看，人格发展的进步将由整体自己识别并觉察其所有自我意识部分的能力所决定。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Until you are honest with yourself and become consciously aware of yourself, you cannot honestly relate with others; you will project upon them your own fears and prejudices. You cannot afford to help them because you have too many insecurities within yourself. Now, you form the physical reality that you know, individually and en masse. To change your world you must change your thoughts. You must become consciously aware of what you tell yourself is true every moment of the day, for that is the reality that you project outward.
quotes.translation: 除非你对自己诚实并有意识地觉察自己，否则你无法真诚地与他人相处；你会将自己的恐惧和偏见投射到他们身上。你无法帮助他们，因为你内心有太多不安全感。现在，你们个体和集体地形成了自己所知的物质实相。要改变你的世界，你必须改变你的思想。你必须每时每刻有意识地觉察你告诉自己为真的事物，因为那正是你向外投射的实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In ways almost impossible to describe, the ground rules were not as yet firmly established. Gravity itself did not carry its all-pervasive sway, so that the air was more buoyant. Man was aware of its support in a luxurious, intimate fashion. He was aware of himself in a different way, so that, for example, his identification with the self did not stop where his skin stopped: He could follow it outward into the space about his form, and feel it merge with the atmosphere with a primal sense-experience that you have forgotten.
quotes.translation: 以几乎无法描述的方式，基本规则尚未牢固建立。重力本身并未发挥其无所不在的支配力，因此空气更为轻盈。人类以奢侈而亲密的方式觉察到它的支撑。他以不同的方式觉察自己，例如，他对自己的认同并不止于皮肤边界：他能将这种认同延伸至形体周围的空间，并感受它与大气融合——这是一种你们已遗忘的原初感官体验。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner ego is a portion of the self, for example -- is the portion of your self -- that is aware of your reincarnational activities. It is the part of you that exists outside of time, yet simultaneously lives in time. You form your own reality. The ego that you are aware of obviously could not form your body for you, however, or grow your bones. It knows how to assess the conditions of the world. It makes deductions. Your reasoning is highly important, yet alone it cannot pump your blood or tell your eyes how to see.
quotes.translation: 内在自我是"自己"的一部分——例如——是你们自己中觉察到转世活动的那部分。它是存在于时间之外、同时又活在时间中的你。你们形成自己的实相。然而，你们所觉察的自我显然无法为你塑造身体或生长骨骼。它知道如何评估世界状况。它进行推论。你们的推理能力非常重要，但仅凭它无法泵送血液或告诉眼睛如何观看。

quotes.text: Through the years a history of personal projections will serve to prove to you the nonphysical aspect of the self. You may even meet me （smile, eyes open wide） in some future projection. My meetings with you here involve to some extent projections on my part and on Ruburt's part. As yet he is not consciously aware of these projections. When I am here, he is often somewhere else. Part of him remains to keep check on me, and unfortunately this is the part that could be, but is not as yet, aware of his other surroundings.
quotes.translation: 多年来个人投射的历史将向你们证明自己的非物质面向。你们甚至可能在未来的某次投射中遇见我（微笑，睁大眼睛）。我与你们的会面在某种程度上涉及我自身和鲁柏的投射。目前他尚未有意识地觉察这些投射。当我在这里时，他常常在别处。他的一部分留下来监视我，不幸的是，这部分本可觉察他的其他环境，但目前尚未做到。

quotes.text: （When Seth is speaking rapidly I find that I write rapidly in order to keep up, often without much awareness of what I am putting down as far as meaning goes. Because of this I occasionally miss out on asking what may seem like obvious questions. A case in point in the above delivery would be the passage where Seth states: "Now Ruburt helped me, though consciously he was not aware of this, because of the peculiar circumstances." I would have liked this explained at the time. As it is it must wait for the next session.
quotes.translation: 当赛斯快速说话时，我发现我也得快速书写才能跟上，往往不太清楚自己写下的内容在意义上是什么。正因如此，我偶尔会错过提出那些看似显而易见的问题。在上述讲述中，一个明显的例子就是赛斯说：“现在，鲁柏帮助了我，尽管在意识上他并未察觉，因为情况特殊。”我当时很想请赛斯解释这一点。但现在只能等到下一节课了。

quotes.text: In greater terms you are just as awake when you are asleep, but the focus of your awareness is turned in other directions. As you know, you can live for years while in a coma, but you could not live for years without ever sleeping. Even in a coma there is mental activity, though it may be impossible to ascertain it from the outside. A certain kind of free conscious behavior is possible when you are not physically oriented as you are in the waking state, and that activity is necessary even for physical survival.
quotes.translation: 从更广义的角度来说，你在睡眠时同样清醒，只是你的意识焦点转向了其他方向。如你所知，人可以在昏迷状态下活上多年，但若从不睡眠，却无法存活多年。即使在昏迷中，仍有心智活动，尽管从外部可能无法确认。当你不像清醒状态那样以肉体为导向时，某种自由的有意识行为是可能的，而这种活动即使对肉体的生存也是必要的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

Resume, finally, at 11:25.)

quotes.text: "Imagine a body with a fully operating body consciousness, not diseased or defective, but without the overriding ego-directed consciousness that you have. The sleepwalkers' physical abilities surpassed yours. They were as agile as animals, their purpose simply to be. Their main points of consciousness were elsewhere, their primary focuses scarcely aware of the bodies they had created. Yet they learned 'through experience,' and began to 'awaken,' to become aware of themselves, to discover time, or to create it.
quotes.translation: “想象一个拥有完全运作的身体意识的身体，没有疾病或缺陷，但缺乏你所拥有的主导性自我意识。梦游者的身体能力超越了你。他们像动物一样敏捷，他们的目的仅仅是存在。他们的意识主要点在其他地方，主要焦点几乎不察觉他们创造的身体。然而，他们‘通过经验’学习，并开始‘觉醒’，开始意识到自己，发现时间，或创造时间。

source_raw.title: 2. Seth first discussed the "sleepwalkers" in Volume 2 of "Unknown" Reality--see Session 708 for September 30, 1974. Here's a much-condensed version of what he told us that night after break ended at 9:56:

quotes.text: He is another aspect of me while being himself. (Pause.) In your terms, I am a guide he also follows. He is much more aware of our relationship however than you are of your relationship to him. (Pause; well over one minute long.) Your time is required... These communications change me as they change you, for they are action on both of our parts. Your egos are being well educated, since they are aware of the sessions, and this will be of great benefit to you when other coordinates come into your perception.
quotes.translation: 他是我的另一个面向，同时又是他自己。（停顿。）用你们的说法，我是他也追随的向导。然而，他对我们关系的觉察远比你对自己与他关系的觉察要深。（停顿；超过一分钟。）你们的时间是需要的……这些交流改变了我，也改变了你们，因为这是我们双方的行动。你们的自我正在受到良好的教育，因为它们意识到了这些课程，当其他坐标进入你们的感知时，这将给你们带来巨大的益处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Emotion and imagination are being considered as far superior. The displaced powers of consciousness are still being assigned to the unconscious, and great efforts are being made to reach what seem to be normally inaccessible areas of awareness. To this end drugs are utilized, cults set up, and there are methods and training manuals galore. Period. Yet there is nothing basically inaccessible about such "inner knowledge or experience." It can all be quite conscious, and utilized to enrich the reality that you know. The conscious mind is not some prodigal child or poor relative of the self. It can quite freely focus into inner reality when you understand that it can. You, again, have a conscious mind. You can change the focus of your own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 情感和想象力被视作远为优越。被取代的意识力量仍被归入无意识，人们正付出巨大努力去触及那些看似通常无法触及的意识领域。为此，药物被使用，教派被建立，还有大量的方法和训练手册。句号。然而，这种“内在知识或经验”本质上并非无法触及。它们完全可以被有意识地利用，以丰富你所知的实相。有意识的心智并非自我的浪子或穷亲戚。当你理解它可以时，它可以相当自由地聚焦于内在实相。再次强调，你拥有一个有意识的心智。你可以改变自己意识的焦点。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You would know better than to think that your awareness of the movie signified that the movie began only when you appeared. You construct dreams automatically, so to speak, with one level of your being in the main, whether you wake or sleep, and whether you are aware of the dreams or not. This is an inner symbol manipulation that is carried out as automatically as you breathe or walk, a by-product of your own physical and psychic structure, of your chemical and electromagnetic constitution.
quotes.translation: 你应更明白，不会认为你对电影的察觉意味着电影只在你出现时才开始。你可以说，无论你醒着还是睡着，无论你是否意识到梦境，你都在用你存在的一个主要层面自动构建梦境。这是一种内在符号的操纵，其自动性如同你呼吸或行走，是你自身身体与心灵结构、化学与电磁构成的副产品。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: If, however, you learn to know yourself better in daily life to become more fully aware even of your earthly life, then you will indeed receive other information that hints of a deeper, more supportive reality, in which physical existence rests. You will find yourself having experiences that do not fit recognized facts. These can add up to an alternate set of facts, pointing toward a different kind of reality, and give evidence for an inner existence that takes precedence over the physical assumptions. A certain kind of discretion and understanding is necessary, however. Basically the inner reality is the creative source of the physical one. Yet to some extent, the physical rules are also inviolate -- at their level.
quotes.translation: 然而，若你在日常生活中学会更深入地认识自己，甚至对尘世生活也变得更加觉知，那么你确实会接收到其他信息，这些信息暗示着一个更深层、更具支持性的实相——物质存在正栖身于其中。你会发现自己的经历与公认事实不符。这些经历可以累积成另一套事实，指向另一种实相，并为内在存在提供证据——这种内在存在优先于物质假设。不过，某种程度的审慎与理解是必要的。基本上，内在实相是物质实相的创造源头。然而在某种程度上，物质规则在其层面也是不可侵犯的。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You begin to manipulate the dream state, or rather you begin to manipulate yourself within dream reality, which is something different. It is an entirely new environment. In ordinary dreaming, without the awareness of usual waking consciousness, perception of dream reality is limited and instinctive. All in all you get along quite well. You are like a child in objective reality. When you learn to take waking awareness into the dream condition, you are reaching adolescence, so to speak.
quotes.translation: 你开始操控梦境状态，更确切地说，你开始在梦境实相中操控自己——这是截然不同的事。这是一个全新的环境。在普通梦境中，由于缺乏通常清醒意识的觉知，对梦境实相的感知是有限且本能的。总体而言，你应付得相当不错。你就像客观实相中的孩子。当你学会将清醒意识带入梦境状态时，可以说你正在步入青春期。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Present and future experience of those in the room are available to me, and as real as their present experience. Therefore I must remember what they think has already happened, or not yet occurred, for to me it is one. These patterns of activity, however, are also constantly changing. I say for example that I am aware of their past and future actions and thoughts; and yet what I am aware of, actually, are ever-shifting and changing patterns, both in the future and in the past.
quotes.translation: 房间里众人当下与未来的经验对我而言都是可及的，其真实性不亚于他们当下的经验。因此我必须记住他们认为已经发生或尚未发生的事——因为对我而言，它们是一体的。然而这些活动模式也在不断变化。举例来说，我声称能感知他们过去与未来的行动和思想；但实际上，我所感知的是未来与过去中不断流转变化的模式。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Ruburt keeps track of intuitive details that neither of you are even conscious of, and so there shows an integrity that at least sometimes he is not aware of. You yourselves adopt personalities, though usually you are not aware of doing so. So I adopt a personality that can communicate with your own.33 In one manner of speaking mine is heroic, larger, and multidimensional. On the other hand, I can operate only mentally in your world. It is Ruburt who must walk down the street.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏追踪着连你们自己都未曾意识到的直觉细节，因此展现出一种至少有时连他自己都未曾察觉的完整性。你们自己也在扮演人格，尽管通常并未意识到这一点。所以我采用一个能与你们人格沟通的人格。从某种角度说，我的人格是英雄式的、更宏大的、多维度的。另一方面，我只能以心智方式在你们的世界运作。真正需要走上街头的，是鲁柏。

Precognitively you are unconsciously quite aware -- again in your terms -- of the results of any given action or cause. quotes.text: Its own stability and awareness can be vastly deepened and strengthened. In times of seemingly calamitous encounters with nature, individuals may find themselves amazed at their capacity to relate with other people, but in the artificially induced psychic disaster area of massive LSD therapy, the situation is reversed. Consciousness finds itself in a crisis situation; not [because of one coming] from the exterior world, but because it is forced to fight on a battleground for which it was never designed and cannot understand, where basically counted-upon allies of association, memory and organization, and all the powers of the inner self, are suddenly turned into enemies.
quotes.translation: 其自身的稳定性与觉知可以大大深化和强化。在看似与自然灾难性遭遇的时刻，个体可能会惊讶于自己与他人建立联系的能力；但在大规模LSD治疗这种人为诱导的心理灾难区域，情况则相反。意识发现自己处于危机情境——并非因为来自外部世界的威胁，而是因为它被迫在一个从未被设计去理解、也无法理解的战场作战，在那里，原本可依赖的联想、记忆、组织等盟友，以及内在自我的所有力量，突然都变成了敌人。

quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Some of these may involve the thoughts of what you would call a reincarnational self, focused in another period of history as you know it. You may instead, "pick up" an event in which a probable self is involved, according to your inclination, your psychic suppleness, your curiosity, your desire for knowledge. In other words, you may become aware of a far greater reality than you now know, use abilities that you do not realize you possess, know beyond all doubt that your own consciousness and identity is independent of the world in which you now focus your primary attention. If all of that were not true, I would not be writing this book and you would not be reading it.
quotes.translation: 其中一些可能涉及你们所谓的转世自我的思想，聚焦于你们所知历史中的另一个时期。你反而可能根据你的倾向、你的心灵柔韧度、你的好奇心、你对知识的渴望，"捕捉"到一个涉及可能自身体验的事件。换句话说，你可能会意识到一个远比你现在所知更广阔的现实，运用你尚未意识到自己拥有的能力，确凿无疑地知道你的意识和身份独立于你目前主要关注的世界。如果这一切不是真的，我就不会写这本书，你也不会读它。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... True order and organization, even of biological structure, can be achieved only by granting a basic unpredictability. I am aware that this sounds startling. Basically, however, the motion of any wave or particle or entity is unpredictable -- freewheeling and undetermined. Your life structure is a result of that unpredictability. Your psychological structure is also. However, because you are presented with a fairly cohesive picture, in which certain laws seem to apply, you think that the laws come first and physical reality follows. Instead, the cohesive picture is the result of the unpredictable nature that is and must be basic to all energy.
quotes.translation: 给我们一点时间……真正的秩序和组织，即使是生物结构的，也只能通过承认基本的不可预测性来实现。我知道这听起来令人震惊。但基本上，任何波、粒子或实体的运动都是不可预测的——自由自在、未确定的。你的生命结构正是这种不可预测性的结果。你的心理结构也是如此。然而，由于你面对的是一个相当连贯的画面，其中似乎适用某些法则，你认为法则先存在，物质实相随后产生。相反，这个连贯的画面是那种必须作为所有能量基础的不可预测本性的结果。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are, again, no limitations to the self, but those that you create. These become valid limitations for all practical purposes however. When you learn, and you are learning, to extend the limitations of your self, you simply become aware of what you are already. You become more aware, but there is always more to become aware of.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，自我没有限制，只有你创造的限制。然而，这些限制在一切实际目的中变得有效。当你学习——你正在学习——扩展自我的限制时，你只是意识到你早已是的那个自己。你变得更加觉察，但总有更多可以觉察的。

quotes.text: In your terms they did not have [an overall] purpose, yet their purpose was simply to be. Their main points of consciousness were elsewhere, in another kind of reality, while their physical manifestations were separate. Their primary focuses of consciousness were scarcely aware of the bodies they had created. Yet even those bodies learned, in quotes now, "through experience," and began to "awaken," to become aware of themselves, to discover time, or to create it. Period.
quotes.translation: 用你们的说法，它们没有[整体]目的，然而它们的目的仅仅是存在。它们的主要意识点位于别处，在另一种实相中，而它们的物质显化是分离的。它们意识的主要焦点几乎不知道它们所创造的身体。然而，即使是那些身体，也通过"经验"学习（现在引号内），并开始"觉醒"，开始意识到自己，发现时间，或创造时间。句号。

quotes.text: You are aware, alert, and participating in many more realities than you know as your soul expresses itself through you. That consciousness of your usual daylight hours, the ego consciousness, rises up like a flower from the ground of the "underneath," the unconscious bed of your own reality. Though you are not aware of it, this ego itself emerges, then falls back again into the unconscious, from which another ego then rises as a new bloom from the springtime earth.
quotes.translation: 你正觉察、警觉并参与许多比你已知更多的实相，因为你的灵魂通过你表达自己。你通常白天的意识，即自我意识，像花朵一样从"底层"的地面——你自己实相的无意识床——升起。虽然你没有意识到，但这个自我本身浮现，然后又落回无意识中，另一个自我随之升起，就像春天大地上的新花。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Tuesday does not exist to the inner ego. Your outer ego is now experiencing the camouflage time year of 1964. This simply does not exist to the inner ego, therefore the inner ego is effortlessly aware of any choices or decisions that you may make freely in say 1970. It does not affect your choice. It has absolutely nothing to do with whatever future choice you may make. It is aware of such future choices simply because the camouflage future does not exist for it.
quotes.translation: 星期二对内在自我并不存在。你的外在自我现在正在经历1964年的伪装时间年。这对内在自我根本不存在，因此内在自我毫不费力地意识到你在比如1970年可能自由做出的任何选择或决定。它不影响你的选择。它与你可能做出的任何未来选择完全无关。它意识到这些未来选择，仅仅是因为伪装未来对它并不存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Because of the great organizing nature of these basic units, there are also psychological structures that are quite capable of holding their own identities while being aware of any given number of probable selves. Life after death has great meaning in your reality, because death is a part of it. Your greater reality obviously transcends both your births and your deaths. The idea of one universe alone is basically nonsensical. Your reality must be seen in its relationship to others.8 Otherwise you are always caught in questions like "How did the universe begin?" or "When will it end?" All systems are constantly being created.
quotes.translation: 由于这些基本单位具有强大的组织性，也存在着能够保持自身身份认同、同时觉察到任意数量可能自我的心理结构。死后生命在你们的实相中具有重大意义，因为死亡是实相的一部分。你们更大的实相显然超越了你们的出生与死亡。单一宇宙的概念根本就是荒谬的。你们的实相必须放在与其他实相的关联中看待，否则你们永远会被"宇宙如何起源？"或"它何时终结？"这类问题所困。所有系统都在不断被创造中。

quotes.text: (To me): Now you early foresaw your part in these sessions, and our work. One of the paintings you did many years ago clearly foreshadowed the development of your psychic endeavors. This was the one you sold, of the man that hung for some time in the position in which my portrait now hangs. That was a portrait of Joseph; in other words of your own inner identity as you intuitively perceived it at the time. You were not consciously aware of the connection, but you were consciously aware of the painting's strong impact.
quotes.translation: （对我而言）：你早已预见到自己在这几堂课中的角色，以及我们的工作。你多年前创作的一幅画，清楚预示了你心灵能力的发展。就是你卖掉的那幅画，画中人物曾悬挂在我肖像现在的位置好一阵子。那是约瑟的肖像——换句话说，是你当时凭直觉感知到的内在身份。你并未有意识地觉察到其中的关联，但你确实有意识地感受到那幅画强烈的冲击力。

quotes.text: From your viewpoint such a disintegration is, of course, not pleasant, but as the personality loses its focus on your plane it gathers itself together on another plane, and such a gradual gathering together is much more favorable than the surprise of a complete and sudden departure. Already Miss Callahan's vital core of awareness is appearing on another plane, and if you will forgive an analogy, she appears there as a wondering but not frightened young girl.
quotes.translation: 从你们的视角来看，这样的解体当然令人不悦，但当人格失去对你们层面的聚焦时，它会在另一层面重新聚合。这种渐进的聚合过程，远比突如其来的完全离去更有利。卡拉汉小姐的意识核心已经出现在另一层面，请容我打个比方：她在那里的形象就像一个充满好奇却不恐惧的年轻女孩。

quotes.text: Many writers of historical pieces, for example, are writing out of direct experience with those times. Such instances represent an excellent working rapport between the present self and the unconscious, which brings these memories to the surface in such a way that current life is enriched. More often than not, true awareness of the situation often becomes almost conscious, and just beneath awareness the individual knows the source of his authentic material.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，许多历史题材的作家，其实是在书写对那个时代的直接经验。这类案例展现了当下自我与潜意识之间极佳的工作默契，使得这些记忆浮现到意识表层，从而丰富当下的生命。通常，对这种情况的真实觉察几乎会达到意识层面，而在觉察之下，个体清楚知道其真实材料的来源。

quotes.text: The freshness of dream experience lies in its direct nature. Your cultural world view does not have any clear understanding of the nature of dreams, so that their direct, clear expression is not recalled often in the morning. (Pause.) At night you tune in to dreaming reality simply by closing out so-called waking reality, but the same kind of dream experience continues beneath your focus in waking life. Dreaming, you are still aware of your daily experience, but it is seemingly peripheral. Waking, your dream experience is peripheral also, but you are less aware of that condition. Both together represent the dimensions of your consciousness, and they exist simultaneously. You can and often do work out in dreams the challenges of daily life. In waking life you are also working out challenges set for yourselves in the dream state. Obviously, then, your consciousness is equipped to function in the known and unknown realities, and the divisions that you have set up are quite arbitrary.
quotes.translation: 梦境经验的新鲜感在于其直接性。你们的文化世界观对梦的本质缺乏清晰理解，因此梦的直接而清晰的表达往往不被晨起时记起。（停顿）夜晚，你只需屏蔽所谓的清醒实相，就能调频进入梦境实相，但同样的梦境经验在你清醒时的焦点之下持续进行。做梦时，你仍能觉察到日常经验，但它似乎处于边缘。清醒时，你的梦境经验也处于边缘，但你对这种状态觉察较少。两者共同代表了你意识的维度，且同时存在。你可以在梦中解决日常生活的挑战，也确实经常如此。在清醒生活中，你也在解决梦境中为自己设定的挑战。显然，你的意识具备在已知与未知实相中运作的能力，你所设定的区分完全是武断的。

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Selfhood overspills with great luxurious outcroppings, yet you jealously guard against such creativity. To a certain extent you do carry the knowledge of your forefathers within your [cells'] chromosomes,1 which present a pattern that is not rigid but flexible -- one that in codified fashion endows you with the subjective living experience of those who, in your terms, have gone before. As Ruburt recently suspected, some very old cultures have been aware of this.2 Period. While being independent individuals their members also identified with their ancestors to some extent, accepting them as portions of their selfhoods. This does not mean that the individual self was less, but was more aware of its own reality. A completely different kind of focus was presented, in which the ancestors were understood to contribute to the "new" experience of the living; one in which the physically focused consciousness clearly saw itself as perceiving the world for itself, but also for all of those who had gone before -- (gradually louder for emphasis:) while realizing that in those terms he or she would contribute as well as the generations past.
quotes.translation: 稍等片刻……自我身份以丰沛的涌现方式满溢而出，但你们却小心翼翼地防范这种创造力。在某种程度上，你们确实将祖先的知识携带在[细胞]染色体中，这些染色体呈现的是一种非僵化而具弹性的模式——以编码方式赋予你们那些（用你们的话说）已逝者的主观生活经验。正如鲁柏最近所怀疑的，某些古老文化早已意识到这一点。句号。这些文化的成员在保持独立个体的同时，也在某种程度上与祖先认同，将祖先视为自我身份的一部分。这并不意味着个体自我变得薄弱，而是更清楚地觉察到自身的实相。这里呈现了一种完全不同的焦点：祖先被认为对生者的"新"经验有所贡献；在这种焦点中，聚焦于物质世界的意识清楚看见自己不仅为自己感知世界，也为所有已逝者感知世界——（逐渐提高音量以强调：）同时明白，以那些术语而言，他或她也将如过往世代一样做出贡献。

quotes.text: Once scientists theorized the ether as the medium in which the physical universe existed.1 Framework 2 is the psychological medium in which the consciousness of the world exists. The word "ego" is much bandied about, and in many circles it has a poor reputation. It is, however, as I use it, a term meant to express the ordinarily conscious directive portion of the self. It is your conscious version of what you are -- an excellent description, if I do say so myself (with amusement). It is directed outward into the physical world. It is also aware, however, of some of your "unconscious" activities. It is the you you identify with, so it is as aware of your dreams, for example, as you are, and it is quite conscious of the fact that its existence rests upon knowledge that it does not itself possess.
quotes.translation: 科学家曾假设以太是物理宇宙存在的介质。框架二是世界意识存在的心理介质。"自我"这个词被频繁使用，在许多圈子里名声不佳。然而，在我使用它时，这是一个用来表达自我通常有意识指导部分的术语。它是你对自己有意识的版本——如果我自己这么说的话，这是一个极好的描述（带着幽默）。它向外指向物质世界。然而，它也意识到你的一些"无意识"活动。它是你认同的那个你，所以例如它和你一样意识到你的梦境，并且它很清楚它的存在依赖于它本身并不拥有的知识。

quotes.text: The solution therefore may not necessarily be accepted completely as the best solution by the ego. The ego is aware of the physical situation only in its relationship to itself. It is not aware of the internal situation. The solution however must be made in relation to the total conditions. It is extremely important then that the ego correctly interprets the physical situation, for this is the information that it will give to the computer, so to speak.
quotes.translation: 因此，解决方案可能不一定被自我完全接受为最佳方案。自我只在与自身相关的情况下意识到物质情境。它没有意识到内在情境。然而，解决方案必须与整体条件相关联。因此，自我正确解读物质情境极为重要，因为可以说，这是它将输入计算机的信息。

quotes.text: In other words the ego is not familiar with the probable self, but certain portions of the subconscious are. The subconscious is aware of many realities which are not accepted by the ego as actual physical events, and it reacts to many stimuli of which the ego is completely unaware.

quotes.text: It is very difficult for them to understand your ideas of past, present and future. Now give us a moment. (No real pause.) quotes.translation: 他们很难理解你们对过去、现在和未来的概念。现在请给我们一点时间。（没有真正的停顿。）在其他维度，或者如果你更喜欢在其他发展阶段，多维人格意识到它的主要身份，同时也意识到它送入许多实相、进入可能系统的分支人格，因为它追求其本性中固有的所有可能行为和创造。

quotes.translation: 这些投射需要强大的推进力，因为你实际上是从一个维度或实相旅行到另一个。然而你将保持觉知。今晚我一直在谈论保持觉知的投射，而不是通常被遗忘的更频繁的投射。

quotes.text: There was no point at which consciousness was introduced, because consciousness was the illumination from which the first cells emerged. That illumination was everywhere then at every point aware of itself, and of the conditions formed by its presence. In your terms each species is aware of the conditions of each other species, and of the entire environment. In those terms the environment forms the species and the species forms the environment.
quotes.translation: 意识被引入的节点并不存在，因为意识是第一个细胞出现时的光芒。那光芒当时无处不在，在每一点上意识到自身，以及由其存在所形成的条件。用你们的说法，每个物种都意识到其他物种的条件以及整个环境。用那些说法，环境形成物种，物种形成环境。

quotes.text: That inner, all-pervasive existence becomes known to the extent that you grow more responsive to your own inner environment. This does not mean that you become entirely self-centered, blind to the rest of the world. It does not mean that you must meditate for hours, or study your own thought processes with such vigor that you ignore other activities. It simply means that you are aware of your own life as clearly as possible -- in touch with your thought processes, aware of them but without overdue concern or overanalysis. They are as much a part of your inner environment as trees are of your exterior world. There are different species of selves in the same fashion. There are different species of worlds.
quotes.translation: 那内在、遍及一切的存在，会随着你对自身内在环境愈发敏感而变得可知。这并非意味着你变得完全以自我为中心，对世界其他部分视而不见。也不意味着你必须长时间冥想，或过度钻研自己的思维过程以致忽略其他活动。它仅仅意味着你尽可能清晰地觉察自己的生命——与自己的思维过程保持联结，觉察它们，但不过度担忧或过度分析。它们是你内在环境的一部分，正如树木是你外在世界的一部分。同样地，自我也有不同种类，世界也有不同种类。

quotes.translation: 你会更强烈地被那些在某种程度上强化你当前人生的"前世"所吸引。你意识到自己早期的记忆是稀疏的。大多数人几乎不记得婴儿和孩童时期的岁月。你运用那时获得的知识，虽然它已成为你的一部分，但你并未有意识地觉察到它；同样地，你也没有有意识地觉察到其他转世存在。（低声，幽默地）：新段落。

quotes.text: The awareness is focused along certain lines. The energy is likewise focused. Much of the ability again is suspended as for a subject in a trance, but consciousness is present. Your hybrid plants merely demonstrate this susceptibility to new suggestion which your plant, like your susceptible trance subject, will gladly follow. I will have more to say along this road of thought, but am detoured for just a moment as to which fork to follow.
quotes.translation: 觉察力沿着特定路线集中。能量也同样集中。许多能力再次被悬置，如同处于出神状态的受试者，但意识依然存在。你的杂交植物只是展示了这种对新暗示的易感性，你的植物会像易受暗示的出神受试者一样欣然遵从。关于这条思路，我还有很多要说的，但暂且绕道片刻，决定该走哪条岔路。

quotes.text: In these sessions the full range of brain waves is utilized as you understand them. Here, in a highly creative, disciplined, and yet spontaneous performance, a situation is set up in which knowledge is obtained from the known frequencies, combined so that consciousness can use itself more fully, reaching into many areas closed to one range of consciousness alone. The various diverse, unique characteristics of each level of awareness are given play. In a way this is like an accelerated, chosen, well-organized "conscious" dream venture, in which Ruburt travels through mediums of consciousness until finally he, still being himself, is nevertheless no longer himself (humorously), but me.
quotes.translation: 在这些课程中，你所理解的脑电波全波段都被运用。在这里，以一种高度创造性、有纪律却又自发的方式，设置了一种情境，从已知的频率中获取知识，加以组合，使意识能更充分地运用自身，触及仅凭单一意识范围无法触及的许多领域。每个觉察层次的各种多样、独特的特性都得以发挥。在某种程度上，这就像一次加速的、经过选择的、组织良好的"有意识"梦之旅，鲁柏在其中穿越意识的媒介，直到最终他仍然是他自己，却也不再是他自己（幽默地），而是我。

quotes.text: For example: There was no beginning, and there will be no end, yet parables have been given telling you of beginnings and endings simply because with your distorted ideas of time, beginnings and endings seem to be inseparable, valid events. As you learn to turn the focus of your attention away from physical reality and therefore experience some slight evidence of other realities, your consciousness will cling to old ideas that make true explanations impossible for you to understand. Multidimensional awareness is available to you in your dreams, however, in some trance states, and often even beneath ordinary consciousness as you go about your day.
quotes.translation: 例如：没有开始，也没有结束，但寓言却告诉你们开始和结束，仅仅是因为以你们扭曲的时间观念来看，开始和结束似乎是不可分割的有效事件。当你们学会将注意力的焦点从物质实相转移开，从而体验到其他实相的一些微弱证据时，你们的意识会紧抓着旧观念不放，这使得真正的解释对你们来说无法理解。然而，多维度的觉察力在你们的梦中、在某些出神状态中，甚至在你们日常活动时普通意识之下，都是可及的。
