As your ability grows in relation with the environment of your plane then you can afford to look around, use the inner senses, and enlarge your scope of activity. This is only natural. Survival on a particular plane depends upon your concentration in that plane. Again, when survival is more or less satisfied by attention then you can avail yourself of the opportunity to turn your attention elsewhere.
quotes.translation: 这是一种心灵焦点，是沿特定方向的觉知凝聚。当你与所在层面环境相关的能力增长时，你便能环顾四周，运用内在感官，扩大活动范围。这纯属自然。在特定层面的生存取决于你对该层面的专注。再次强调，当注意力基本满足生存需求后，你便有机会将注意力转向别处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The action and counteraction is the time trigger. To you this will be almost unbelievable, but on some other planes motion is simultaneous and time unknown. To me time can be manipulated, used at leisure and examined. To me your time is a vehicle, one of the several vehicles by which I can enter your awareness. It is therefore still a reality of some kind to me. Otherwise I could not utilize it in any manner whatsoever. As an example of my good intentions I will end this session.
quotes.translation: 作用与反作用即是时间触发器。对你而言这几乎难以置信，但在某些其他层面，运动是同时发生的，时间并不存在。对我而言，时间可以被操控、随意使用并加以检视。对我而言，你的时间是一种载具——是我能进入你觉知的几种载具之一。因此它对我而言仍具有某种实相性。否则我根本无法以任何方式利用它。作为我善意的一个例证，我将结束此节。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I do not make it a practice to observe. We are connected, however, in our psychological gestalt, and so I am aware of any intense feelings on your part, or strong reactions of any kind. This does not mean that I am necessarily aware of all events within your lives, or that I always break down the feelings I receive from you into specifics.
quotes.translation: 我并非习惯性地进行观察。然而，在我们的心理完形中我们彼此相连，因此我能感知到你任何强烈的情绪或任何形式的强烈反应。这并不意味着我必然知晓你生活中的所有事件，或总能将接收到的感受分解为具体细节。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When you are born, you understand that you have a new consciousness. You explore its ramifications. It is your primary evidence that you exist in flesh. Basically, each person must confront the experience of reality through a direct encounter with it. This encounter takes place through the use of the physical senses, of course, as they are used to perceive and interpret physical data. The very utilization of those senses, however, is dependent upon the nature of your consciousness itself, and that consciousness is aware of its power and action through the exercise of its own properties.
quotes.translation: 当你出生时，你明白自己拥有全新的意识。你探索它的衍生脉络。这是你存在于肉身中的首要证据。从根本上说，每个人都必须通过直接遭遇来面对现实的体验。这种遭遇自然通过运用物质感官实现——这些感官被用来感知和解读物质数据。然而，这些感官的运用本身取决于你意识的性质，而该意识通过行使自身属性来觉察其力量与行动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In one respect, and in quite a legitimate and objective manner, all thoughts are also projections. They leave you in an objective actual manner, and exert an objective effect. They are a part of your psychic identity projected outward, yet you do not feel any loss. Nor are you aware of what happens to these thoughts. You could not retain simultaneously all and exert all the thoughts of your lifetime thus far in any normally conscious way. To do so would be psychological suicide.
quotes.translation: 从某个相当合理且客观的角度来看，所有思想也都是投射。它们以客观真实的方式离开你，并产生客观效应。它们是你向外投射的心灵身份的一部分，但你却感觉不到任何损失。你也不清楚这些思想的去向。你无法以任何正常意识状态同时保留并运用此生迄今的所有思想。若强行如此，无异于心理上的自杀。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: I am also quite aware of the cruelty that would be involved if I led you on in this endeavor without due consideration. I can only tell you that I appreciate both your objectivity and your beliefs. I will do my best, my dear doctor, to satisfy you in both respects. You may call it chance. You may call it if you choose coincidence. You may name it in whatever way pleases you: nevertheless, it is because of my personal rapport with you that I will bother with any displays at all.
quotes.translation: 我也很清楚，若我在未充分考量下引导你进行这项尝试，其中将涉及何等残酷。我只能说，我既欣赏你的客观性，也尊重你的信念。亲爱的医生，我会尽力在两方面都让你满意。你可以称之为机缘，也可以称之为巧合，或任何你喜欢的名称：但无论如何，正是因为我与你的个人默契，我才愿意费心进行任何展示。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The core of the self is obviously aware of all realities to some extent. If portions of the self did not coincide then it would be impossible for the whole personality to ever operate as a unit. Here we simply have spirals, so to speak, of ever moving actions that compose the whole self. But portions of these spirals coincide, and in this analogy the spirals of action not only have those dimensions which you understand, but other dimensions with which you are not yet familiar.
quotes.translation: 自我的核心显然在某种程度上知晓所有实相。若自我的部分彼此不重合，那么整个人格就永远无法作为整体运作。这里我们只需想象构成完整自我的、不断运动的行动螺旋。但这些螺旋的部分区域相互重合，在此类比中，行动螺旋不仅具有你所理解的维度，还包含你尚未熟悉的其它维度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Such a change of focus demands a concentration in one area to the exclusion, as a rule, of other areas. In the waking state you are usually not aware of your dreaming existence. In your dreaming existence your physical environment is replaced by your dream environment. You react to both environments whenever you are concentrated within them. Our study of the dream state will take us into many other discussions, for you will learn much about the nature of uncamouflaged reality.
quotes.translation: 这种焦点的转变通常要求将注意力集中于某一领域而排除其他领域。在清醒状态时，你通常意识不到自己的梦境存在。在梦境存在中，你的物质环境被梦境环境所取代。当你专注于其中任一环境时，你都会对其产生反应。我们对梦境状态的研究将引向许多其他讨论，因为你会从中了解大量关于非伪装实相的本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You are becoming aware of higher levels of your own personality and you are trying to interpret them. The sensation was your own reaction to the psychological presence of your friend Ferd. He did not cause the sensations. But in another way he was their cause, since you interpreted his existence in that particular manner. Because of your own personality makeup you will lean to physical interpretations, and physical sensationsin an attempt to understand nonphysical impressions.
quotes.translation: 你正在逐渐觉察到自己人格的更高层面，并试图诠释它们。那种感受是你对朋友费德心理存在的个人反应。他并未引发这些感受，但从另一层面而言他又是其肇因——因为你以那种特定方式诠释了他的存在。由于你自身的人格特质，你会倾向于用物质层面的诠释和身体感受来理解非物质性的印象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The inner ego is indeed the "I" of your dreams, having somewhat the same sort of position within the inner self as the ego itself has to the physical self. Actions however merge within the inner ego. The scope of awareness is more complex. We will at a later time discuss this inner ego in connection with the dream situation and health.
quotes.translation: 内在自我确实是你们梦境中的"我"，它在内在自体中的位置，恰如自我在物质自体中的位置。然而行动在内在自我中相互融合，觉察的范围更为复杂。我们稍后将结合梦境情境与健康议题来探讨这个内在自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The conscious physically oriented portions of the self become acquainted with the inner information. To some extent the reality of thought is consciously perceived as the innovator behind physical matter. Such an individual then can understand the nature of hallucinations at the point of death, and with full conscious awareness enter into the next plane of existence. The information made conscious is then passed on to others where it can be physically recognized and applied.
quotes.translation: 自我中面向物质意识的部分逐渐熟悉内在信息。在某种程度上，思想的实相被有意识地感知为物质现象背后的创新者。这样的个体因而能理解临终幻觉的本质，并以全然清醒的意识进入下一个存在层面。被意识化的信息随后传递给他人，使其能在物质层面被认知和应用。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: For example, the main character in a religious historical drama may or may not consciously be aware of the ways in which such information is given to him. And yet it may seem to him that he does know, for the nature of a dogma's origin will be explained in terms that this main character can understand. The historical Jesus knew who he was, but he also knew that he was one of three personalities composing one entity. To a large extent he shared in the memory of the other two.
quotes.translation: 例如，宗教历史剧中的主角可能未必有意识地知晓这些信息传递给他的方式。然而他或许自认为知晓，因为教义起源的本质会以他能理解的术语得到解释。历史上的耶稣知道自己是谁，但他也明白自己是构成一个存有的三个人格之一。在很大程度上，他共享着另外两位的记忆。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To a different degree it is filled with conscious semi personalities. I call them semi personalities merely to point out that they are not as developed as you are, as you are not as developed as your entity is. Nevertheless, that dream world experiences continuity. It is not aware of any break while, for example, you are sleeping. It does not know you sleep or wake. It merely exists to a fairly vivid degree while you sleep, and it sleeps but does not die while you are awake.
quotes.translation: 那个世界在不同程度上充满了有意识的半人格。我称其为半人格，仅是为了指出它们不如你们这般发展完善，正如你们不及自己的存有那般发展完善。尽管如此，那个梦境世界仍经历着连续性。例如当你沉睡时，它觉察不到任何中断。它不知晓你的睡眠或清醒。当你入睡时它仅以相当鲜明的程度存在，而当你清醒时它则沉眠却不会消亡。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: None of the above mentioned are aware of your plane. There is among others another classification beside culture establishment existences, and these keep camouflage patterns at a minimum and are not as similar as cultural establishment existences are to your own plane. These materialize camouflage pattern only at certain times to express and deal with inner conflicts not yet worked out. I did not intend to get into this so deeply yet, as it may be confusing to say the least.
quotes.translation: 上述存在均未觉察到你们的层面。除了文化建制存在之外，还存在另一种分类，它们将伪装模式维持在最低限度，且与你们层面相似度不及文化建制存在。这些存在仅在特定时刻物质化伪装模式，以表达和处理尚未解决的内在冲突。我本无意如此深入探讨此话题，因为至少可以说这可能会造成混淆。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: We are all existing in many dimensions at once. The primary difference between us is that I am aware of my existence in many dimensions, and you are not aware of yours. We form a new dimension in our sessions. I give voice to ideas that are known to various levels of your personalities, yet I am not at the level of your personalities.
quotes.translation: 我们同时存在于许多维度中。我们之间的主要区别在于，我意识到自己存在于多个维度，而你尚未意识到自己的多维存在。我们在课程中形成了一个新的维度。我为你人格各层面所知的理念赋予声音，但我并不处于你人格的层面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: I admit that a birthday operates as a handy reference. But if you realized that your consciousness did exist before that time, your memory will open up, and your accepted birthdate will appear far less important. "Coming out of the womb" is an event, and much better to use than "birth." In greater terms -- far greater terms than you imagine -- you are aware of probable "births," and your other parentages [that are] quite as legitimate as the personal history you now accept.
quotes.translation: 我承认生日是一个方便的参照点。但若你意识到自己的意识在出生之前就已存在，你的记忆将会开启，而你所接受的出生日期将显得不那么重要。"脱离子宫"是一个事件，比"出生"一词更贴切。从更宏大的层面——远比你想象的更宏大——你意识到各种"可能的出生"，以及同样合法的其他亲缘关系，它们与你现在所接受的个人历史同样真实。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 729
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You may not be aware of the conscious nature of each atom in your body, or of the gestalt consciousness formed by those atoms as they build into cells, but it is not necessary for them, or for their own awareness of themselves, that you give them that recognition. The consciousness in the cells exists whether or not you recognize it.
quotes.translation: 你可能未意识到体内每个原子的意识本质，也未意识到这些原子构建成细胞时形成的整体意识，但你的认知对它们或它们对自身的觉知并非必要。无论你是否承认，细胞中的意识始终存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The atoms that compose the fetus have their own kind of consciousness. The volatile awareness-consciousnesses that exist independently of matter, form matter according to their ability and degree. The fetus, therefore, has its own consciousness, the simple component consciousness made up of the atoms that compose it. This exists before any reincarnating personality enters it. The consciousness of matter is present in any matter -- a fetus, a rock, a blade of grass, a nail.
quotes.translation: 构成胎儿的原子拥有自己的意识。那些独立于物质存在的易变觉知-意识，会根据自身能力与程度塑造物质。因此胎儿拥有自己的意识——由构成它的原子组成的简单成分意识。这存在于任何转世人格进入之前。物质的意识存在于任何物质中——胎儿、岩石、草叶、指甲。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In your world you identify as yourself only, and yet love can expand that identification to such an extent that the intimate awareness of another individual is often a significant portion of your own consciousness. You look outward at the world not only through your eyes, but also, to some extent at least, through the eyes of another. It is true to say, then, that a portion of you figuratively walks with this other person as he or she goes about separate from you in space.
quotes.translation: 在你的世界里，你只将自己视为自我，但爱能将这种认同扩展到如此程度，以至于对另一个个体的亲密觉知常常成为你自身意识的重要部分。你不仅通过自己的眼睛向外看世界，至少在一定程度上也通过他人的眼睛。因此可以说，当这个人与你空间分离时，你的一部分象征性地与他同行。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the predream state you directly encounter a reality in which those probabilities exist all at once to your perception. In a dazzling display you are aware of such events from infinite perspectives. Consciously you could not grasp such information, much less act upon it, nor could you maintain your particular, unique, psychological stance. You still take advantage of that level of being, however, using that immeasurable data as a basis to form the reality that you know.
quotes.translation: 在梦前状态中，你直接遭遇一个实相——在那里所有可能性同时呈现于你的感知中。在令人目眩的展现中，你从无限视角觉知这些事件。意识层面你无法把握如此信息，更遑论据此行动，也无法维持你独特、特定的心理立场。然而你仍利用那个存在层面，以那不可估量的数据为基础，构建你所知的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The present personality cannot travel to the complete entity, again because of these very real laws; that is, while caught in or under the influence of the field of your plane, the personality cannot travel to the entity. The personality can and should be aware of the whole self of which it is a part, and that part of the whole self which the ego ignores, is the one part which can enter into the field influence of the entity, though it cannot travel to the entity itself.
quotes.translation: 当前人格无法前往完整的存有，同样是因为这些非常真实的法则；也就是说，当受困于或处于你们层面场域的影响下时，人格无法前往存有。人格能够且应该觉察到它作为一部分的整个自己，而整个自己中自我忽略的那一部分，正是能够进入存有场域影响的那一部分，尽管它无法前往存有本身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: All layers of the personality are indeed conscious layers. They simply operate in the manner of compartments, so that often one portion of the self is not aware of other portions. As a rule when you are awake you do not know your sleeping self; you know your neighbors far better, so your sleeping self appears mysterious indeed. When you are awake, as Ruburt himself has written, you cannot find the dream locations that have been so familiar to you only the night before.
quotes.translation: 人格的所有层次确实都是有意识的层次。它们只是以隔间的方式运作，因此自我的某一部分常常无法觉察到其他部分。通常当你清醒时，你并不了解你的睡眠自我；你对邻居的了解远胜于此，因此你的睡眠自我确实显得神秘。正如鲁柏自己所写的，当你清醒时，你无法找到前一晚还那么熟悉的梦境地点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: This, temporarily, is something like what happened to you, Joseph. The inner self is aware of other realities. It is aware of the ego. Remember here the difference between consciousness of self and the ego, for the difference is important. The ego is but part of the self, part of the conscious self, but focused in one direction.
quotes.translation: 约瑟，这暂时有点像发生在你身上的情况。内在自我觉察到其他实相。它觉察到自我。请记住这里自我意识与自我之间的区别，因为这种区别很重要。自我只是自己的一部分，是意识自我的一部分，但聚焦于一个方向。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now: Bring all of those sensations together. Try to be aware of all of them at once, so that one adds to the others. If you find yourself being more concerned with one particular perception, then make an attempt to bring the ignored ones to the same clear focus. Let all of them together form a brilliant awareness of the moment.
quotes.translation: 现在：将所有那些感觉汇集在一起。试着同时觉察它们全部，让一个感觉叠加到其他感觉上。如果你发现自己更关注某个特定的感知，那么就努力将那些被忽略的感知带到同样清晰的焦点上。让它们全部一起形成对当下的灿烂觉察。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Many limitations are set upon personalities by their own entities for karmic reasons. I want to make it plain that free will does exist, but that it is limited through use of a more extended free will on the part of the entity. Also, the inner ego is aware of so-called future decisions not because he forces such decisions upon the outer ego but simply because the future as such does not exist to the inner ego, and therefore he can perceive where the outer ego cannot.
quotes.translation: 许多限制是由它们自己的存有出于业力原因加在人格上的。我想清楚地说明，自由意志确实存在，但它是通过存有那一方更扩展的自由意志的使用而受到限制的。此外，内在自我觉察到所谓的未来决定，不是因为它将这些决定强加于外在自我，而仅仅是因为未来作为如此对内在自我并不存在，因此它能够看到外在自我所不能看到之处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Practically speaking you see, if you were aware of the constant barrage of telepathic communications that do impinge upon you, it would be most difficult to retain identity. Identity would suffer if it were forced to perceive more impressions than it could effectively handle. Shared dreams are therefore well beneath awareness.
quotes.translation: 实际上，你看，如果你觉察到不断冲击你的心灵感应交流的持续轰炸，那么保持身份认同将极为困难。如果身份认同被迫感知超过它有效处理能力的印象，它就会受损。因此，共享梦境远在意识觉察之下。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Some of this is difficult to verbalize. The EE units2 within matter, within the atoms and molecules, are aware of the probable fields of action that are possible. While the body's integrity must lie in a constant reiteration in one probability, and maintain within that probable system a certain "constant," and while physically perception is largely directed there, the basic integrity of the body system and consciousness comes from outside the system into it. Period.
quotes.translation: 其中有些部分难以用言语表达。物质、原子和分子内的EE单位，觉察到可能存在的行动场域。虽然身体的完整性必须不断重复于一个可能性中，并在那个可能系统内维持某种"常数"，虽然物质感知主要导向那里，但身体系统与意识的基本完整性，是从系统之外进入系统之内的。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 694
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: At some inconceivable point all of the threads would be in turn traversed. Self A, now on thread A, would not be aware in his present, of the quote "future" selves on the other threads. Only by meeting one of these other selves however can he become aware of the nature of this strange structure through which he is traveling.
quotes.translation: 在某个不可思议的节点，所有线索都将被依次穿越。自我A，此刻在线索A上，在其当下无法觉察到其他线索上所谓的"未来"自我。唯有通过遇见这些其他自我之一，他才能意识到自己正在穿越的这个奇异结构的本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I am well aware that the book raises many more questions than it presents answers for, and this has been my intent. The unknown reality will become known to the extent that you form new questions, and forget the old frameworks in which answers and myths were automatically given in response. If this book "works," then many old questions will be seen as relatively meaningless, formed not after any intimate encounter with basic issues, but in response to old dogmas.
quotes.translation: 我深知这本书提出的问题远多于它给出的答案，而这正是我的意图。未知实相将在你形成新问题、遗忘那些自动给出答案与神话的旧框架时，逐渐为你所知。如果这本书"奏效"，那么许多旧问题将被视为相对无意义——它们并非源于对基本议题的亲密接触，而是对旧教条的反应。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 743
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Ruburt instinctively likes your tree painting. It represents a certain state of consciousness -- an in-between threshold dimension of awareness, in which the imagination and the senses are almost caught in the act of putting an object together, or of bringing the world into a sensed reality, brand-new, from the realm of the inner mind: a very evocative state of consciousness, and one that as I believe Ruburt mentioned, you could also use in connection with faces.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏本能地喜欢你的树画。它代表某种意识状态——一个介于中间的阈限觉知维度，在此维度中，想象力与感官几乎被捕捉到正在将物体组合起来，或将世界从内在心灵领域带入全新的感官实相：一个极具唤起力的意识状态，正如我相信鲁柏提过的，你也可以将其用于面容的描绘。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Previous ego patterns can still operate under various circumstances. Ruburt was often a male, and he sometimes attempts to manhandle the feminine aspects of his present personality, to inflict upon it literally qualities that go against its grain. Force, logic over intuition, for example, or intellect over psychic awareness . The psychic abilities in his case are, among other things, aided by that balance of characteristics, and these should not be tampered with.
quotes.translation: 先前的自我模式仍可在各种情况下运作。鲁柏常是男性，他有时试图粗暴对待其当前人格的女性面向，强行赋予其违背本性的特质。例如，力量凌驾直觉，或理智压制心灵觉知。在他的案例中，通灵能力尤其得益于这些特质的平衡，这些不应被篡改。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: On the other hand as I have told you, your past continually changes. It does not appear to change to you, for you change with it. You are not aware of the changes that have come about. The question of clairvoyance however is not at issue with information given concerning your past. Your future changes as your past changes. But clairvoyant information deals with the future, that is, precognitive clairvoyant information, and it is here that the issue shows itself.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，正如我告诉过你的，你的过去持续在改变。它在你看来并未改变，因为你随之改变。你并未觉察到已发生的改变。然而，关于你过去的信息所涉及的预知问题，并非关键所在。你的未来随你的过去而改变。但预知信息涉及未来——即预知性的通灵信息——而问题正是在这里显现出来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: All systems of reality are created, or constructed, by those who perceive the system. Those who do not construct a system cannot perceive it. Now. There is nothing at all unusual in precognitive experiences. They occur constantly beneath the level of your awareness. However there are certain conditions necessary before precognitions can arise to conscious levels, and there are definite conditions that must exist before what you call apparitions can be perceived.
quotes.translation: 所有实相系统都是由感知该系统的那些人所创造或建构的。不建构系统的人便无法感知它。现在。预知经验中毫无异常之处。它们持续发生在你的意识层面之下。然而，预知要上升到意识层面必须具备某些条件，而你们所谓的幻影能被感知之前，也必须有明确的条件存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Now, probabilities exist in the same manner. They simply are not concrete for you, as dreams are not concrete for you. You may dream for example of holding an apple, then wake up and the apple is gone. This does not mean that the apple did not exist. It simply means that in the waking state you are not aware of its reality. You do not experience it, or perceive it. In the same manner you do not perceive or experience physically the actuality of probable events.
quotes.translation: 现在，可能性以同样的方式存在。它们只是对你而言不具体，就像梦境对你而言不具体一样。例如，你可能梦见自己拿着一个苹果，然后醒来苹果就不见了。这并不意味着苹果不存在。它只是意味着在清醒状态下你意识不到它的实相。你没有体验它，也没有感知它。同样地，你也没有在物质层面感知或体验可能事件的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause.) The greatest scientific discoveries are always "accidents." They come from intuitional creativity, when suddenly a new kind of significance is seen that was not "earlier" predictable. You accept all data that fit your theories, and ignore clues to the contrary. Yet underneath it all you are significance-making creatures, pattern-formers, immersed in time but basically apart from it, and so new insights come into your awareness and literally change the quality of any given reality at any given time.
quotes.translation: （停顿一分钟。）最伟大的科学发现总是"意外"。它们来自直觉性的创造力，那时突然看到一种"先前"无法预测的新意义。你接受所有符合你理论的数据，而忽略相反的线索。然而在这一切之下，你们是创造意义的存在、模式构建者，沉浸于时间之中却基本上独立于时间之外，因此新的洞见会进入你的意识，并在任何特定时刻真正改变任何特定实相的性质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 694
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Your own idea of the perilous nature of existence becomes so strong that the ego allows this data to emerge, even though it is "out of time," because your fearful beliefs convince it that you must be on guard. The incidents do not even have to involve you. From all the unconscious telepathic and clairvoyant data available, however, you will be aware of this particular grouping, and it will only serve to reinforce your idea that existence is above all perilous.
quotes.translation: 你自己关于存在之危险本质的想法变得如此强烈，以至于自我允许这些数据浮现，即使它们"不合时宜"，因为你恐惧的信念说服你必须保持警惕。这些事件甚至不必涉及你本人。然而，从所有可用的无意识心灵感应和千里眼数据中，你会意识到这个特定的组合，而这只会强化你的观念：存在首先是危险的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I will let you know very shortly what is happening if you are not aware yourself of contact. Conceivably he could make contact with you in the past, and you would have forgotten, but this is not the case. Although both of you realities are physical ones, the environments are entirely different. The forms you make out of matter are only generally similar. His consciousness has been perceiving this sort of unfamiliar environment with some awe. (Meaning our own.)
quotes.translation: 如果你自己没有意识到接触的发生，我会很快让你知道正在发生什么。可以想象，他可能在过去与你接触过，而你已经忘记了，但事实并非如此。尽管你们两人的实相都是物质性的，但环境却完全不同。你们用物质构成的形态只有大致相似。他的意识一直带着某种敬畏在感知这种不熟悉的环境。（指我们的环境。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: It is your loss. Now, there is a way--when you are involved in physical activity, and even intense mental work--to change the inner focus so that you are aware of two realities at once and can manipulate quite easily in the physical reality as you must. Now, you ask your Bega, for I have told him the message, and I have told him to tell you. We shall see how good he is. And I want to know what he tells you for I want to see how he is delivering my suggestions.
quotes.translation: 这是你的损失。现在，有一种方法——当你从事身体活动，甚至进行高强度脑力工作时——可以改变内在焦点，使你同时意识到两种实相，并能如常轻松地在物质实相中运作。现在，你去问你的贝加，因为我已经把讯息告诉了他，并让他转告你。我们来看看他有多称职。我想知道他告诉了你什么，因为我想看看他是如何传达我的建议的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: When you find yourself as alert, responsive, and intellectual in the dream state as you are in waking life, it becomes impossible to operate within the old framework. This does not mean that in all dreams that particular kind of awareness is achieved, but it is often accomplished within the suggested wake-sleep pattern.
quotes.translation: 当你发现自己处于警觉、敏锐且理智的状态，既在梦境中也在清醒时，便无法再以旧框架运作。这并非意味着所有梦境都能达到这种特定觉知，但在建议的醒睡模式中，这种状态常常得以实现。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: An almost automatic determination must be set up however if projections with conscious awareness are to be anything but rare oddities. With you both the problem is somewhat different than it might be with others. These chemical excesses are used up for one thing in your own creative work. You do this now automatically.
quotes.translation: 然而，若要让有意识觉知的投射不再只是罕见奇事，就必须建立近乎自动的决心。对你们两人而言，这个问题与其他人略有不同。这些化学过剩物质，其一会在你们的创作工作中被消耗。你们现在已自动如此运作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: I am in somewhat the same position when I try to explain to you the nature of this inner self, for while you can become aware of it in dreams, you cannot truly appreciate its maturity or abilities; yet they are yours in the same way that the man's abilities belonged to the child. In the dream state you learn, among other things, how to construct your own physical reality day by day, just as after death you learn how to construct your next physical lifetime.
quotes.translation: 当我试图向你们解释内在自性的本质时，我处于类似的境地——因为你们虽能在梦中觉知它，却无法真正领会其成熟度或能力；然而它们属于你们，正如那人的能力属于孩童一般。在梦境状态中，你们学习如何日复一日构建自己的物质实相，正如死后你们学习如何构建下一世物质生命。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... The universe is seeded with various kinds of consciousnesses. Some of these appear to you as planets or stars,8 as they "intrude" into your field of actuality. As such they appear to behave in a certain fashion, to take a certain form, to have certain effects. You and the stars are simultaneous events, each conscious and aware but in different "scales" of actuality -- as your scale of consciousness is different from that of the violets.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……宇宙中播撒着各种意识。有些意识在“侵入”你们的实相场时，显现为行星或星辰。它们以特定方式运作，呈现特定形态，产生特定效应。你们与星辰是同时性事件，各自具有意识与觉知，但处于不同的实相“尺度”——正如你们的意识尺度与紫罗兰不同。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 729
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Now. It is true that much of our work is done outside of session time. Session time often represents the end result of our work. In projections other portions of Ruburt's personality leave your system. They are not portions needed for physical manipulation. The ego is not even aware of them. There is nothing particularly unusual in this. The unusual aspect, comparatively speaking, is that the information gained can be translated and used within your system.
quotes.translation: 现在。确实，我们大部分工作都在课程时间之外完成。课程时间往往代表我们工作的最终成果。在投射中，鲁柏人格的其他部分会离开你们的系统。这些部分并非肉体操控所需。自我甚至对它们毫无察觉。这并无特别之处。相对而言，不寻常之处在于，所获信息能被翻译并在你们的系统中使用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: You must therefore understand and examine your beliefs, realize that they form your experience, and consciously change those that do not give the effects you want. In such an examination you will be aware of many excellent beliefs that work for you. Trace these through. See how they were followed by your imagination and emotions. If possible, look in your own past for points where recognizable new ideas came to you and beneficially changed your experience.
quotes.translation: 因此，你们必须理解并检视自己的信念，认识到它们塑造了你们的经验，并有意识地改变那些无法带来预期效果的信念。在此检视中，你们会意识到许多对你们有效的卓越信念。追溯这些信念的脉络，观察它们如何被你们的想象力与情感追随。若有可能，回顾自身过往，找出那些带来可辨识新观念并有益改变你们经验的转折点。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is true that on a conscious level you do not as yet operate outside of time, but are bound by it. When you learn to free yourselves from those dimensions to some extent, you are not simply duplicating or "returning" to some vaster condition, but adding a new element to that condition. The kind of self-awareness you have is unique, but all kinds are unique. Each triumph you make as an individual is reflected in your species and in its cellular knowledge.
quotes.translation: 确实，在意识层面上，你们尚未能脱离时间运作，仍受其束缚。当你们学会在一定程度上摆脱这些维度时，并非单纯复制或"回归"某种更宏大的状态，而是为那种状态增添新元素。你们拥有的自我觉知是独特的，但所有形式的觉知都是独特的。你们作为个体取得的每一次胜利，都会反映在你们的物种及其细胞知识中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 705
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: In larger terms, in the predream states you are aware of all of the activities of your own greater psyche as it participates in -- and contributes to -- the infinite existence of psychic consciousness as you understand it, and becomes aware of the psychological realities that form the framework of its own stability.
quotes.translation: 从更宏观的角度看，在梦境前状态中，你们能觉察自身更大心灵的所有活动——当它参与并贡献于你们所理解的心灵意识的无限存在时，并意识到构成其自身稳定框架的心理实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Beneath these, so to speak, the CU's (or units of consciousness) are aware of the different kinds of consciousness of which they are part. By their nature certain kinds of organization, behavior, and experimentation exclude other quite-as-valid but different approaches. The CU's, in their freewheeling nature beneath all matter, are acquainted with all such organizations, so that some of the lessons learned by one species are indeed transferred to another.
quotes.translation: 可以说，在这些之下，CU（意识单位）能觉察到它们所隶属的不同意识类型。按其本质，某些组织、行为与实验方式会排斥其他同样有效但不同的途径。CU在其超越所有物质的自由流转本性中，熟悉所有这类组织，因此一个物种习得的某些经验确实会传递给另一个物种。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 690
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: This process continues beneath conscious and even subconscious awareness. It is not dependent upon the ego, but predates it. These are the first and the last images. The individual cells are the first animals possessing innate awareness of their environment with rudimentary sensemechanisms, to your way of thinking.
quotes.translation: 这个过程持续存在于意识甚至潜意识觉知之下。它不依赖自我，而是先于自我存在。这些是最初与最终的意象。个体细胞是最初的动物，以你们思维方式而言，它们凭借原始感知机制拥有对环境的天生觉知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Your own physical environment appears as it does to you because of your own psychological structure. If you gained your sense of personal continuity through associative processes primarily, rather than as a result of the familiarity of self moving through time, then you would experience physical reality in an entirely different fashion. Objects from past and present could be perceived at once, their presence justified through associative connections. Say that your father throughout his lifetime has eight favorite chairs. If your perceptive mechanisms were primarily set up as a result of intuitive association rather than time sequence, then you would perceive all of these chairs at one time; or seeing one, you would be aware of the others. So environment is not a separate thing in itself, but the result of perceptive patterns, and these are determined by psychological structure.
quotes.translation: 你们所处的物质环境之所以呈现为当前模样，源于你们自身的心理结构。如果你们主要通过联想过程而非自我在时间中移动的熟悉感来获得个人连续性，那么你们将以完全不同的方式体验物质实相。过去与现在的物体可能同时被感知，其存在通过联想连接得以合理化。假设你父亲一生有八把心爱的椅子，若你的感知机制主要基于直觉联想而非时间序列，那么你会同时感知所有这些椅子；或看到一把时便意识到其他椅子。因此环境本身并非独立存在，而是感知模式的结果，而这些模式由心理结构决定。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In our next chapter I will speak of these in some detail. For now it is sufficient to realize that specific steps, definite alterations, occur as consciousness is shifted from the exterior to the interior reality, and that these changes are not random; that consciousness leaves through a very predictable route to its many destinations. Through the ages the Speakers have taught dreamers how to manipulate in these other environments. They have taught them how to bring back information that could be used for the good of the present personality. According to the intent, present purpose, and development, an individual may be aware of these travels to varying degrees. Some have excellent recall, for example, but often misinterpret their experience because of conscious ideas.
quotes.translation: 下一章我将详细讨论这些。目前只需明白：当意识从外在实相转向内在实相时，会发生特定步骤与明确转变，且这些变化并非随机；意识会经由一条可预测的路径抵达其众多目的地。历代传讯者教导做梦者如何在这些其他环境中运作，教导他们带回可用于裨益当前人格的信息。根据意图、当下目的与发展程度，个体可能对这些旅程有不同程度的觉知。例如，有些人有极佳的记忆，却常因意识观念而误解自身经验。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "You" presently have a once-centered consciousness, in that "you" close off from your experience these other stages of consciousness in which other portions of your entire identity are intimately involved. These other stages of consciousness create their own realities as you create your own. The realities are, therefore, byproducts of consciousness itself. If you could become aware of these, they might appear to be other places to you, rather than realms or fields of different kinds of activities. If you probe into these realms you will be forced to perceive them with the root assumptions of your own system, translating feelings of warmth and comfort, for example, into images of warm shelters or buildings, or feelings of fear into images of demons.
quotes.translation: 你"目前拥有一个以自我为中心的觉知，因为你"从自身经验中屏蔽了其他意识阶段，而你整体身份的其他部分正密切参与其中。这些其他意识阶段创造着各自的实相，正如你创造自己的实相。因此，实相本身就是意识的副产品。如果你能觉察到它们，它们在你眼中可能更像是"其他地方"，而非不同活动的领域或场域。若你深入探究这些领域，你将被迫用自己系统的根本假设去感知它们——例如，将温暖舒适的感受转化为庇护所或建筑物的意象，或将恐惧转化为恶魔的意象。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When I come here to speak I focus my energy, but not toward this room as a destination, for in your terms this room does not exist to me. In your terms, this room does not even exist to you. You pretend to agree that it does; we meet in no place of space or time. The true meetings that take place here have nothing to do with the room or the people that you think you are. You know that you hallucinate the room, that you are as much in trance here as you ever are when you are in psychological time. I simply want you to realize that if this life is a trance, then you can turn the direction of your consciousness to perceive greater realities that presently exist. You can be aware of your own greater identity, even as I am. You sit within the miracle of yourselves and ask for signs. It is your inner eyes I would open.
quotes.translation: 当我前来讲话时，我会聚焦能量，但并非以这个房间为目标——因为就你们而言，这个房间对我并不存在。就你们而言，这个房间甚至对你们也不存在。你们假装认同它存在；我们在时空之外的某处相遇。此处发生的真实会晤与房间或你自以为是的"人"毫无关联。你明白自己正在幻化这个房间，此刻你处于出神状态的程度，与你在心理时间中时并无二致。我只想让你意识到：若此生是一场出神，你便能扭转意识方向，去感知当下存在的更宏大实相。你能够觉察到自身更大的身份，正如我这般。你端坐于自身的奇迹之中，却向外寻求征兆。我真正想开启的是你的内在之眼。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are two ways to get at your own conscious beliefs. The most direct is to have a series of talks with yourself. Write down your beliefs in a variety of areas, and you will find that you believe different things at different times. Often there will be contradictions readily apparent. These represent opposing beliefs that regulate your emotions, your bodily condition and your physical experience. Examine the conflicts. Invisible beliefs will appear that unite those seemingly diverse attitudes. Invisible beliefs are simply those of which you are fully aware but prefer to ignore, because they represent areas of strife which you have not been willing to handle thus far. They are quite available once you are determined to examine the complete contents of your conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 有两种方法可以触及你自身的显意识信念。最直接的方式是进行一系列自我对话。写下你在各个领域的信念，你会发现自己在不同时刻相信着不同的事物。矛盾之处往往显而易见，这些代表着你相互对立的信念——它们调控着你的情绪、身体状况与物质体验。检视这些冲突，那些看似分歧的态度背后会浮现出隐形信念。隐形信念不过是你完全知晓却宁愿忽视的部分，因为它们代表着迄今你尚未愿意处理的冲突领域。一旦你决心检视显意识心智的全部内容，它们便唾手可得。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: When you are determined to manipulate your environment, then you separate yourself from it. Since you are part of it, this also leads you to try to place yourself apart from your own subjective reality. It is quite possible to take your normally conscious "I" into the dream state, to your advantage. When you do this you will see that the dreaming "I" and the waking "I" are one, but operating in entirely different environments. Therefore, you become familiar with depths of experience and knowledge unknown to you before. You acquire a true flexibility and expanded awareness of your own being, and open channels of communication between your waking and dreaming realities. This means that you are far better able to utilize unconscious knowledge, and also to acquaint the unconscious with your present physical situation.
quotes.translation: 当你决心操控环境时，你便将自己与环境分离。既然你是环境的一部分，这也会导致你试图将自身与主观实相割裂。将你通常清醒的"我"带入梦境状态是完全可行的，且对你有益。当你这样做时，你会看见做梦的"我"与清醒的"我"本为一体，只是运作于完全不同的环境。因此，你将熟悉此前未知的经验与知识深度。你获得真正的灵活性，扩展对自身存在的觉知，并开启清醒实相与梦境实相之间的沟通渠道。这意味着你能更有效地运用无意识知识，同时让无意识了解你当前的物质处境。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Beyond this there are experiences but seldom recalled, in which the usual identification of your consciousness with physical-life orientation is gone. (Pause.) Images as you think of them are based upon your own neurological structure, and your interpretations of these. When you consider survival after death, for instance, you imagine all the senses fully operating, though perhaps in a nonphysical body. Perception without images seems impossible in that context. Yet in some dream situations you enter a state of awareness quite divorced from that kind of sense data. Images as such are not involved, though later they may be manufactured unconsciously for the sake of translation. In those conditions you come close to an understanding of what your consciousness is when it is not physically oriented at all.
quotes.translation: 超越这些之后，还存在极少被忆起的体验——此时你意识与物质生命导向的惯常认同已消失。（停顿）你所认知的"意象"基于自身的神经结构及你对它们的诠释。例如，当你考虑死后存续时，你会想象所有感官仍在运作——或许是在非物质的躯体中。在此语境下，无意象的感知似乎不可能存在。然而在某些梦境情境中，你会进入一种完全脱离此类感官数据的觉知状态。意象本身并不参与其中，尽管之后它们可能为便于转译而被无意识制造出来。在这些状态中，你接近了理解"当意识完全不具物质导向时它究竟是什么"的真相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The physical reality into which you are born is not nearly as solid or predetermined or definite as it appears to be. Instead there is a field of rich interaction. Your consciousness must be focused at one particular range of frequencies before it can even perceive matter, much less solidity. In sleep your consciousness fluctuates between ranges of intensities, literally flowing into and out of the physical-matter grouping, and forming from more plastic "pre-matter" (with a hyphen) stages, the final shape that matter will take in your world. The same applies to events, where some will be crystallized in physical terms and others will not. The deep portions of your own being are aware of those purposes and intents that are uniquely yours. Unconsciously, then, you have within you what you might think of as a set of blueprints for the particular kind of physical reality you want to materialize. You are the architect.
quotes.translation: 你降生于其中的物质实相，远非表面看起来那般坚固、预定或确定。相反，这里存在着一个丰饶的互动场域。你的意识必须聚焦于特定频率范围，才能感知物质——更遑论坚固性。睡眠时，你的意识在不同强度范围间波动，literally 流入流出物质粒子群，并从更具可塑性的"前物质"阶段，塑造出物质在你们世界中的最终形态。事件亦然——有些会结晶为物质形式，有些则不会。你自身存在的深层部分知晓那些独属于你的目的与意图。因此，在无意识层面，你内在拥有可视为"蓝图"的东西——它描绘着你想要物质化的特定物质实相。你，就是那位建筑师。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Many who write want to develop and use the same abilities, yet it is obvious from their letters that their beliefs prevent them from trusting the inner self enough. You cannot fear your own being and expect to travel through it, to explore its dimensions. First you must take the simple step of affirming your identity. That affirmation will release those attributes that you have and open up new avenues of experience. They will and must be your own. When you ask others to interpret your dreams, for example, you are automatically putting the fulfillment of your own potentials a step away. When you ask another to tell you the direction of your life, then to some extent you keep from yourself the realization that you yourself possess it. Without that awareness no methods will help you.
quotes.translation: 许多想写作的人希望开发并运用同样的能力，但从他们的来信中明显看出，他们的信念阻止了他们足够信任内在自我。你不能恐惧自己的存在，却又期望穿越它、探索它的维度。首先你必须采取简单的步骤来肯定自己的身份。这种肯定将释放你所拥有的那些特质，并开启新的经验途径。这些途径将且必须是属于你自己的。例如，当你请别人解读你的梦境时，你自动将自身潜能的实现推远了一步。当你请别人告诉你人生的方向时，你在某种程度上阻止了自己认识到你本身就拥有这种方向。没有这种觉知，任何方法都帮不了你。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I think it important to periodically remind the reader of certain of Seth's basic ideas throughout both volumes of "Unknown" Reality. As an example, I'll continue with the subject of time -- but Seth's time now -- and couple it with his notions of a durability that is at the same time spontaneous and simultaneous, as he's explained to us more than once. The durability is achieved through constant expansion in terms of value fulfillment. Part of my paragraph of commentary following the 724th session, in Volume 2, fits in here: "As he [Seth] quite humorously commented in the 14th session for January 8, 1964, '... for you have no idea of the difficulties involved in explaining time to someone who must take time to understand the explanation.' Yet Seth's simultaneous time isn't an absolute, for, as he also told us in that session: 'While I am not affected by time on your plane, I am affected by something resembling time on my plane ... To me time can be manipulated, used at leisure and examined. To me your time is a vehicle, one of several by which I can enter your awareness. It is therefore still a reality of some kind to me [my emphasis]. Otherwise I could not utilize it in any way whatsoever.'"
quotes.translation: 我认为定期提醒读者《未知的实相》两卷中赛斯的某些基本观点很重要。举例来说，我将继续探讨时间这个主题——但现在是赛斯的时间——并将其与他关于持久性的概念结合起来，这种持久性同时是自发且同时性的，正如他不止一次向我们解释的那样。这种持久性是通过价值完成方面的持续扩展而实现的。我在第二卷第724节之后的评论段落中有一部分与此相关："正如他（赛斯）在1964年1月8日的第14节中相当幽默地评论道，'……因为你完全不知道向一个必须花时间才能理解解释的人解释时间有多困难。'然而赛斯的同时性时间并非绝对，因为正如他在那一节中也告诉我们的：'虽然我不受你们层面时间的影响，但我受到我层面上某种类似时间的东西的影响……对我来说，时间可以被操控、被悠闲地使用、被审视。对我来说，你们的时间是一种载具，是我进入你们觉知的几种方式之一。因此它对我来说仍然是某种实相（我的强调）。否则我根本无法以任何方式利用它。'"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Every so often I've thought of averaging Jane's dictation time for Seth Speaks and Personal Reality in the same way, but haven't done so. I'm somewhat puzzled to note, however, that her very short working times for the Seth books seem to be either ignored or taken for granted by practically everyone -- or, perhaps, those factors just aren't understood in terms of ordinary linear time. Maybe I'm alone in my interest here, for even Jane doesn't express any great curiosity about the time she has invested in the Seth material; she just delivers it. But given her abilities, I think her speed of production is a close physical approach to, or translation of, Seth's idea that basically all exists at once -- that really there is no time, and that the Seth books, for example, are "there" to be had in final form for just the tuning in. (In Section 3 of this volume, Note 2 for Session 692 contains information on another way by which we can move closer to Seth's idea of simultaneity from our physical reality, but that method grows out of material not discussed here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: I'm more inclined to agree with what Seth told us in the 590th session in Chapter 22 of Seth Speaks: "You are not fated to dissolve into All That Is. The aspects of your personality as you presently understand them will be retained. All That Is is the creator of individuality, not the means of its destruction." And whenever I read about conventional Eastern conceptions of a supreme spirit, I remember what Seth had to say in the 596th session in the Appendix of Seth Speaks: "I have used the term 'expansion of consciousness' here rather than the more frequently used 'cosmic consciousness' because the latter implies an experience of proportions not available to mankind at this time. Intense expansions of consciousness by contrast to your normal state may appear to be cosmic in nature, but they barely hint at those possibilities of consciousness that are available to you now, much less begin to approach a true cosmic awareness."
quotes.translation: 我更倾向于同意赛斯在《赛斯书》第22章第590节中告诉我们的："你并非注定要消融于万有之中。你目前所理解的人格面向将被保留。万有是个体性的创造者，而非其毁灭的手段。"每当我读到传统东方关于至高灵性的概念时，我就想起赛斯在《赛斯书》附录第596节中所说的："我在这里使用了'意识扩展'一词，而非更常用的'宇宙意识'，因为后者暗示了一种人类目前无法获得的经验比例。与你正常状态相比，强烈的意识扩展在本质上可能显得是宇宙性的，但它们几乎无法暗示你现在可获得的那些意识可能性，更不用说开始接近真正的宇宙觉知了。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Every so often I've thought of averaging Jane's dictation time for Seth Speaks and Personal Reality in the same way, but haven't done so. I'm somewhat puzzled to note, however, that her very short working times for the Seth books seem to be either ignored or taken for granted by practically everyone -- or, perhaps, those factors just aren't understood in terms of ordinary linear time. Maybe I'm alone in my interest here, for even Jane doesn't express any great curiosity about the time she has invested in the Seth material; she just delivers it. But given her abilities, I think her speed of production is a close physical approach to, or translation of, Seth's idea that basically all exists at once -- that really there is no time, and that the Seth books, for example, are "there" to be had in final form for just the tuning in. (In Section 3 of this volume, Note 2 for Session 692 contains information on another way by which we can move closer to Seth's idea of simultaneity from our physical reality, but that method grows out of material not discussed here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The CU's, following that analogy, serve as source points or "holes" through which energy falls into your system, or is attracted to it -- and in so doing, forms it. The experience of forward time and the appearance of physical matter in space and time, and all the phenomenal world, results. As CU's leave your system, time is broken down. Its effects are no longer experienced as consecutive, and matter becomes more and more plastic until its mental elements become apparent. New CU's enter and leave your system constantly, then. Within the system en masse, however, through their great and small organizational structures, the CU's are aware of everything happening -- not only on the top of the moment (gesturing), but within it in all of its probabilities.
quotes.translation: 按照这个类比，CU作为源点或"孔洞"而存在，能量通过这些孔洞落入你的系统，或被吸引到你的系统中——并在此过程中形成你的系统。向前时间经验、物理物质在时空中的显现，以及所有现象世界，都是由此产生的。当CU离开你的系统时，时间被分解。其效应不再被体验为连续的，物质变得越来越可塑，直到其心智元素变得明显。新的CU不断进入并离开你的系统。然而，在系统整体内部，通过其大大小小的组织结构，CU觉知到正在发生的一切——不仅是在当下时刻的表面（做手势），而且在其所有可能性之内。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Often, for instance, you handle probabilities very well, while remaining consciously blind to them because of your concepts. Even then, however, on other levels your unconscious reaction will follow your own conscious intents. You may make a move in physical life, for example, seemingly for one reason. You may also be unconsciously reacting to quite pertinent data regarding the probable actions of others. Because you do not really fully accept the fact that you can so react, you may block this unofficial information on the one hand, even while on the other you take it into consideration. You are far more aware than you realize of the probable future in areas with which you are concerned. This is true on all levels. If your purposes do not involve illness, for instance, and yet if you believe in contagion, you will automatically avoid circumstances that can lead to epidemics. In terms of probabilities that particular kind will not enter your experience.
quotes.translation: 例如，你常常能很好地处理可能性，同时由于你的概念而在意识层面对它们视而不见。然而即使如此，在其他层面上，你的无意识反应仍会遵循你自身的有意识意图。例如，你可能会在物质生活中采取一个行动，表面上出于一个原因。你可能也在无意识地对关于他人可能行动的相当相关数据做出反应。因为你并未真正完全接受你能如此反应的事实，你可能一方面阻挡这些非官方信息，另一方面却又将其纳入考虑。在你所关注的领域中，你对可能未来的觉知远比你意识到的要多。这在所有层面上都是如此。例如，如果你的目的不涉及疾病，但你又相信传染，你会自动避开可能导致流行病的情况。在可能性方面，那种特定类型不会进入你的经验。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Because of its position it has great powers of communication, both as a receiver and as a sender. Unfortunately, science as it has developed in your time has resulted in a mistrust of the individual, and saddled him or her with a sense of powerlessness, subjectively, even while it has added a seeming sense of objective power. I say that it has seemingly added a sense of objective power (intently during a fast delivery). For instance, your sophisticated techniques allow you to say that conditions are right for a tornado, and you will have a tornado watch (as we had in our Elmira area not long ago), or your instruments will pick up faint earthquake tremors, and following fault lines you will then "predict" that an earthquake will appear in another area. So it seems that you have some power over your environment. The individual person can then prepare for a potential disaster. It seems that you can seed the clouds with chemicals and bring forth rain when it is needed, and therefore obtain a power over the environment that is quite practical. You believe that you need scientific paraphernalia to achieve such ends -- yet many animals are aware of such phenomena, and without such instruments. And mankind itself is innately equipped to "foresee" such potential disasters.
quotes.translation: 由于其位置，它拥有强大的沟通能力，既作为接收者又作为发送者。不幸的是，在你们时代发展起来的科学导致了对个体的不信任，并给个体带来了主观上的无力感，即使它同时增加了表面上的客观力量。我说它表面上增加了客观力量（在快速讲述中专注地）。例如，你们精密的技术使你们能够说条件适合龙卷风形成，你们就会发布龙卷风警报（就像不久前我们埃尔迈拉地区那样），或者你们的仪器会捕捉到微弱的地震震动，然后沿着断层线你们将"预测"地震将在另一个地区发生。因此，似乎你们对环境拥有某种力量。个体随后可以为潜在灾难做准备。似乎你们可以用化学物质播云，在需要时带来降雨，从而获得对环境相当实用的力量。你们相信需要科学设备才能达到这样的目的——然而许多动物无需这些仪器就能意识到此类现象。而人类本身天生就具备"预见"此类潜在灾难的能力。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: I am simply suggesting that you become more natural. Because science has made an effective barrier to that method of approach, the power seems to reside in the gadgets rather than in man. Man no longer identifies with a storm, for example, and has lost his sense of relationship with it, and therefore his natural power over it. The same applies to storms of the psyche. The dream-art scientist, the true mental physicist, the complete physician -- such designations represent the kinds of training that could allow you to understand the unknown, and therefore the known reality, and so become aware of the blueprints that exist behind the physical universe. The proof is in the pudding, of course. Largely, it seems that your techniques work a good deal of the time. Let us look at medicine, for instance.
quotes.translation: 我只是建议你变得更自然。因为科学已经对那种认知方式形成了有效屏障，力量似乎存在于小装置中而非人类自身。例如，人不再与风暴认同，失去了与它的关系感，因此也失去了对它的自然掌控力。这同样适用于心灵的风暴。梦艺科学家、真正的心理物理学家、完整的医师——这些称谓代表着能让你理解未知、从而理解已知实相、并意识到物质宇宙背后存在蓝图的训练方式。当然，实践是检验真理的标准。大体上，你的方法似乎大部分时间都有效。让我们以医学为例。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now in the case of an animal who hibernates, the body is in the same state. But in the greater hibernation of your own experience, the body as a whole becomes inoperable. The cells within you obviously die constantly. The body that you have now is not the one that you had 10 years ago; its physical composition has died completely many times since your birth, but, again, your consciousness bridges those gaps (with gestures). They could be accepted instead, in which case it would seem to you that you were, say, a reincarnated self at age 7 (intently), or 14 or 21. The particular sequence of your own awareness follows through, however. In basic terms the body dies often, and as surely as you think it dies but once in the death you recognize. On numerous occasions it physically breaks apart, but your consciousness rides beyond those "deaths." You do not perceive them. The stuff of your body literally falls into the earth many times, as you think it does only at the "end of your life."
quotes.translation: 现在，在冬眠动物的情况下，身体处于相同状态。但在你们经验中的更大冬眠里，身体整体变得无法运作。你体内的细胞显然在不断死亡。你现在拥有的身体不是十年前的；自你出生以来，它的物质组成已经彻底死亡多次，但同样，你的意识跨越了那些间隙（伴随手势）。它们也可以被接受，那样的话，你会觉得自己仿佛是七岁（专注地）、十四岁或二十一岁的转世自我。然而，你自身意识的特定序列会贯穿始终。从根本上说，身体经常死亡，其确定性不亚于你认为它在你们所认知的死亡中只死一次。在许多情况下，它会在物质上分解，但你的意识超越了那些"死亡"。你并未感知到它们。你身体的物质实际上多次落入大地，正如你认为它只在"生命终结"时那样。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The other [Saturday] evening while he was in bed Ruburt had a somewhat surprising experience. He was not dreaming. His body was asleep but his consciousness was drifting. He clearly heard my voice. It seemed to come literally from out of the sky, down into another room outside of (next-door to, actually) the one in which his body slept. For a moment the power frightened him, for it sounded like a radio turned up to an incredible degree -- louder than thunder. At the time words were clearly distinguishable, though later he forgot what they said. For an instant he was tempted to interpret the power as anger, for in your world when someone is shouting they are usually angry. He realized, however, that something else was involved. He did not sense my presence, but only heard the thunder of the voice. It shocked him because he is used to hearing my words from within his head -- he had never before been aware of my voice as existing apart from him. In the dream state he has heard me giving him information. In these instances, however, he was the channel through which my voice came. He has often wondered about the nature of my own independence, and the kind of reality in which I exist.
quotes.translation: 上周六晚上，鲁柏躺在床上时经历了一次颇为惊人的体验。他并非在做梦。他的身体睡着了，但意识在飘荡。他清晰地听到了我的声音。那声音仿佛真的从天而降，进入他身体所在房间之外的另一个房间（实际上是隔壁）。那一刻，力量让他感到恐惧，因为它听起来像收音机开到了难以置信的程度——比雷声还响。当时词语清晰可辨，尽管后来他忘记了它们的内容。一瞬间，他倾向于将这种力量解读为愤怒，因为在你们的世界里，当有人大喊时，他们通常很愤怒。然而，他意识到其中涉及别的东西。他没有感知到我的存在，只听到了那声音的雷鸣。这让他震惊，因为他习惯于从自己头脑内听到我的话语——他从未意识到我的声音是独立于他而存在的。在梦境状态中，他听到过我给他信息。然而，在这些情况下，他是我的声音通过的渠道。他常常思考我自身独立性的本质，以及我所存在的实相类型。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What he was sensing, however, was an entirely different kind of reality. He was beginning to recognize another synapse [neuronal] pattern not "native"; he was familiarizing himself with perceptions at a different set of coordinate points. Such activity automatically alters the nature of time in your experience, and is indicative of intersections of your consciousness with another kind of consciousness. That particular type of consciousness operates "at different speeds" than your own. Biologically, your own physical structures are quite able to operate at those same speeds, though as a species you have disciplined yourselves to a different kind of neurological reaction. By altering such neurological prejudice,10 however, you can indeed learn to become aware of other realities that coincide with yours. Period.
quotes.translation: 然而，他所感知的是一种完全不同的实相。他开始识别另一种"非原生"的突触（神经元）模式；他正在熟悉一组不同坐标点的感知。这种活动会自动改变你们经验中时间的本质，并表明你的意识与另一种意识的交汇。那种特定类型的意识以与你不同的"速度"运作。从生物学上讲，你们自身的物理结构完全能够以同样的速度运作，尽管作为一个物种，你们已将自己训练成一种不同的神经反应模式。然而，通过改变这种神经偏见，你确实可以学会意识到与你的实相同时存在的其他实相。句号。

quotes.text: Sit with your eyes open easily, letting your vision take in whatever is before you. Do not strain. On the other hand, do explore the entire field of vision simultaneously. Listen to everything. Identify all the sounds if you can, mentally placing them with the objects to which they correspond even though the objects may be invisible. Sit comfortably but make no great attempt to relax. Instead, feel your body in an alert manner -- not in a sleepy distant fashion. Be aware of its pressure against the chair, for example, and of its temperature, of variations: Your hands may be warm and your feet cold, or your belly hot and your head cold. Consciously, then, feel your body's sensations. Is there any taste in your mouth? What odors do you perceive?
quotes.translation: 轻松地睁眼坐着，让你的视野接纳眼前的一切。不要紧张。另一方面，要同时探索整个视野。倾听一切。如果可以，识别所有声音，在脑海中将它们与对应的物体放置在一起，即使这些物体可能不可见。舒适地坐着，但不要刻意放松。相反，以警觉的方式感受你的身体——不要以困倦疏远的方式。例如，意识到它对椅子的压力，以及它的温度、变化：你的手可能温暖而脚冰冷，或者腹部发热而头部凉爽。然后，有意识地感受你身体的感觉。嘴里有任何味道吗？你感知到什么气味？
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.

quotes.text: The English itself, however, then becomes charged, freshened with new concepts, carrying within a strangeness that itself alters the relationship of the words. This is a dream or trance language. It is as native to its level of consciousness as English is to your own -- or Indian, or Chinese, or whatever. The various focuses of consciousness will have their own "languages." Ruburt has discovered that beneath the Sumari there are deeper meanings.11 He has become aware of what he calls long and short sounds. Some come so quickly that he cannot keep track, or speak them quickly enough. Others are so slow that he feels a sentence would take a week to utter.12 These are the signatures of different focuses of consciousness as they are transposed in your space-time system.
quotes.translation: 然而，英语本身随后会变得充满能量，因新概念而焕然一新，携带着一种陌生感，这本身改变了词语之间的关系。这是一种梦境或出神语言。它对其意识层面来说，就像英语对你们自己的意识层面一样自然——或者印度语、中文，或其他任何语言。意识的各种焦点将拥有它们自己的"语言"。鲁柏发现，在苏马瑞语之下有更深层的含义。他意识到他所谓的短音和长音。有些来得太快，他无法跟上，或无法足够快地说出来。另一些则如此缓慢，以至于他觉得一个句子需要一周才能说完。这些是不同意识焦点在你们时空系统中转译时的特征。

quotes.text: At the same time, EE units (see Note 3) became manifest. I have said, for example, that the universe expands as an idea does, and so the visible universe sprang into being in the same manner. The same energy that gave birth to the universe is, in those terms, still being created. The EE units contain within themselves the latent knowledge of all of the various species that can emerge under those conditions. It is according to your relative position. You can say that it took untold centuries for the EE units to "initially" combine, forming classifications of matter and various species, or you can say that this process happened at once. In your terms, each species is aware of the condition of each other species, and of the entire environment. In those terms the environment forms the species and the species form the environment. There were fully developed men -- that is, of full intellect, emotion, and will -- living at the same time, in your terms, as those creatures supposed to be man's evolutionary ancestors.
quotes.translation: 同时，EE单位（见注3）变得显化。我曾说过，例如，宇宙像观念一样扩展，因此可见宇宙以同样的方式诞生。赋予宇宙生命的相同能量，在这些术语中，仍在被创造。EE单位内在地包含所有能在那些条件下出现的各种物种的潜在知识。这取决于你的相对位置。你可以说，EE单位“最初”结合、形成物质分类和各种物种花费了无数世纪，或者你也可以说这个过程是瞬间发生的。用你们的说法，每个物种都知晓其他物种的状况以及整个环境。在这些术语中，环境塑造物种，物种也塑造环境。有完全发展的人类——即拥有完整智力、情感和意志的人类——在你们的说法中，与那些被认为是人类进化祖先的生物同时存在。

quotes.text: ane and I consider Seth's concept of counterparts to be an intriguing psychological framework, spacious enough to serve as a workable thematic structure in which the social and nationalistic characteristics of our species can be studied, as well as the components of the individual psyche. That is, the private person is here seen as interacting with others because there is, beneath our awareness, an inner "person-to-person" relationship connecting each individual with his or her physical counterparts, though they may well be living in other parts of the globe while sharing the same historical period. It follows, then, that one may or may not ever meet a counterpart "in the flesh" -- may or may not even suspect the existence of such relationships.
quotes.translation: 简和我认为赛斯的对应物概念是一个引人入胜的心理框架，足够宽广，可以作为可行的主题结构，在其中研究我们物种的社会和民族特征，以及个体心灵的组成部分。也就是说，私人个体在此被视为与他人互动，因为在我们意识之下，存在着一种内在的“人对人”关系，将每个个体与他或她的物理对应物连接起来，尽管他们可能生活在全球其他地方，却共享同一历史时期。因此，一个人可能或可能不会在“肉身”中遇到对应物——可能或可能甚至怀疑这种关系的存在。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 721)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Any true psychic development of personality, however, is bound to lead to an understanding of the nature of the psyche that is far too large for any such confusion of basic identity with sexuality. The concept of reincarnation itself clearly shows the change of sexual orientation, and the existence of a self that is apart from its sexual orientation, even while it is also expressed through a given sexual stance. To a good extent, sexual beliefs are responsible for the blocking-out of reincarnational awareness. Such "memory" would necessarily acquaint you with experiences most difficult to correlate with your current sexual roles. Those other-sex existences are present to the psyche unconsciously. They are a portion of your personality. In so specifically identifying with your sex, therefore, you also inhibit memories that might limit or destroy that identification.
quotes.translation: 然而，任何真正的人格灵性发展，必然会导致对心灵本质的理解，这种理解远非任何将基本身份与性取向混淆所能涵盖。转世概念本身清楚地显示了性取向的变化，以及一个独立于性取向的自我的存在，即使它也通过特定的性立场来表达。在很大程度上，性信念阻碍了转世意识的显现。这种“记忆”必然会让你熟悉那些与你当前性角色最难关联的经历。那些异性的存在在无意识层面呈现给心灵。它们是你人格的一部分。因此，当你如此具体地认同你的性别时，你也抑制了可能限制或摧毁这种认同的记忆。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Alter your time orientation in other such exercises. This will automatically allow you to break away from too narrow a focus. It will to some extent break apart the rigid interlocking of your perception into reality as you have learned how to perceive it. Children can play so vividly that they might, for example, imagine themselves parched under a desert sun, though they are in the middle of the coolest air-conditioned living room. They are on the one hand completely involved in their activity, yet on the other hand they are quite aware of their "normal" environment. Yet the adult often fears that any such playful unofficial alteration of consciousness is dangerous, and becomes worried that the imagined situation will supersede the real one.
quotes.translation: 在其他此类练习中改变你的时间取向。这将自动让你摆脱过于狭窄的焦点。它将在某种程度上打破你对现实感知的刚性互锁，正如你学会感知它的方式。孩子们可以玩得如此生动，例如，他们可能想象自己在沙漠烈日下干渴，尽管他们身处最凉爽的空调客厅。一方面他们完全投入于活动，另一方面他们却相当清楚自己的“正常”环境。然而，成年人常常害怕任何这种游戏性的非正式意识改变是危险的，并担心想象的情境会取代真实的情境。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Consciousness within the body knows that its existence is within the body's context, and apart from it at the same time. In ordinary life during the day consciousness often takes a recess, so to speak -- it daydreams, or otherwise experiences itself as somewhat apart from the body's reality. At night, in sleep, the self's consciousness takes longer, freer recesses from physical reality, and does this as spontaneously as the body itself walks. These experiences are not hypothetical. They happen to each person. On such occasions, each person is to some extent aware of a kind of comprehension that is not dependent upon the accumulation of data, but of a deeper kind of experience and direct encounter with the reality from which the world emerges.
quotes.translation: 身体内的意识知道它的存在既在身体的背景下，又同时独立于它。在白天日常生活中，意识常常休息——可以说，它做白日梦，或以其他方式体验自己与身体现实有些分离。在夜晚睡眠时，自我的意识从物理现实中获得更长、更自由的休息，并且像身体走路一样自发地进行。这些体验并非假设。它们发生在每个人身上。在这种时刻，每个人都在某种程度上意识到一种理解，这种理解不依赖于数据的积累，而是依赖于一种更深层次的体验，以及与世界从中涌现的现实直接相遇。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: During their lifetimes animals in their natural state enjoy their vigor and accept their worth. They regulate their own births -- and their own deaths. The quality of their lives is such that their abilities are challenged. They enjoy contrasts: that between rest and motion, heat and cold, being in direct contact with natural phenomena that everywhere quickens their experience. They will migrate if necessary to seek conditions more auspicious. They are aware of approaching natural disasters, and when possible will leave such areas. They will protect their own, and according to circumstances and conditions they will tend their own wounded. Even in contests between young and old males for control of a group, under natural conditions the loser is seldom killed. Dangers are pinpointed clearly so that bodily reactions are concise.
quotes.translation: 在自然状态下，动物在其一生中享受活力并接受自己的价值。它们调节自己的出生——以及自己的死亡。它们的生活质量使得它们的能力受到挑战。它们享受对比：休息与运动、热与冷之间的对比，与无处不在加速它们体验的自然现象直接接触。如果有必要，它们会迁徙以寻求更有利的条件。它们意识到即将到来的自然灾害，并在可能时离开这些区域。它们会保护自己的同类，并根据情况和条件照顾受伤的同伴。即使在年轻和年长雄性之间为控制群体而进行的竞争中，在自然条件下失败者也很少被杀死。危险被清晰地定位，以便身体反应简洁。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Much of this is difficult to explain, again, for information and knowledge is constantly transformed -- almost completely reborn, so to speak, through characteristics that are inherently a part of thought itself. Knowledge is changed automatically through the auspices of each consciousness who perceives it. It is magnified and yet refined. It is a constant language, yet one that transforms itself. When I "attend these sessions," or "speak," then I exchange with others a more complicated system of reality than any computer could handle. You do not understand or perceive the ways in which your reality contributes to the foundation of the mass-world reality that you experience. Unconsciously, each individual participates in forming that world. In my case, however, I am aware of the same kind of activities, only in regard to many realities rather than one.2
quotes.translation: 这些内容大多难以解释，因为信息和知识不断被转化——可以说几乎被彻底重塑，通过思想本身固有的特性。知识会因每个感知者的意识而自动改变，被放大却又被精炼。它是一种恒常的语言，却又不断自我转化。当我"出席这些课程"或"说话"时，我与他人交流的现实系统比任何电脑能处理的都要复杂。你们并不理解或感知到，你们的现实如何为你们所体验的群体世界现实奠定基础。每个个体都在无意识地参与塑造那个世界。然而就我而言，我同样意识到这类活动，只是涉及的是多重现实而非单一现实。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json
